elements.text: ■ Send an exception review request to Freddie Mac if the Servicer determines that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is unable to pay the amount due
elements.label: text

elements.text: As early as 75 days and no later than 60 days before the Mortgage maturity date or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: projected payoff date, the Servicer must take the following actions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer has not established contact with the Borrower, send an additional notice to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower informing the Borrower that the non-interest bearing UPB will become due
elements.label: text

elements.text: and provide the projected payoff date or pending Mortgage maturity date, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the amount of the non-interest bearing UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon establishing contact with the Borrower, discuss with the Borrower that the non￾interest bearing UPB will become due on the projected payoff date or pending Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: maturity date and determine, based on communication with the Borrower, whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is able to pay the non-interest bearing UPB on the maturity date or projected
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoff date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Send an exception review request to Freddie Mac if the Servicer determines that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is unable to pay the amount due
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the initial solicitation but no earlier than 180 days before the maturity date or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: projected payoff date, the Servicer may send additional notices at its discretion leading up to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the maturity date or projected payoff date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Written notification requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: When sending the required written notification set forth above in Section 8504.1(a) to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers regarding the non-interest bearing UPB that is due, the Servicer must refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 1104, Freddie Mac Borrower Non-Interest Bearing Balance Notice. Use of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: exhibit is optional; however, it contains the minimum information that must be conveyed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8504.2: Outreach requirements for Mortgages with
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: buydown plans (11/01/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: As outlined in Section 4204.3, Freddie Mac will purchase Mortgages with temporary subsidy
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown plans (buydown plans). Buydown plans allow the Borrower to benefit from temporary
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsidies of the monthly payment of principal and interest. The buydown plan may not extend
elements.label: text

elements.text: for more than three years after the first scheduled payment date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In coordination with the terms of the buydown agreement and the requirements outlined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4204.3, the Servicer must send notification detailing any respective payment change to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower 90 days prior to the applicable payment change.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8601: Servicing Requirements for Senior
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8601.1: Servicing responsibilities, Borrower
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: interactions and key terms (11/19/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The brown font text used throughout this chapter denotes the special requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages. Text in black or green font denotes general
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements that also apply elsewhere in the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Key terms and definitions used in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s rights and Servicer rights, duties and obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer use of Servicing Tools
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower inquiries
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Key terms and definitions used in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer that receives a notice that it services a Senior Subordinate Mortgage should be
elements.label: text

elements.text: familiar with the following terms.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Senior Subordinate definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: F
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any Mortgage purchased by Freddie Mac that is then
elements.label: text

elements.text: deposited into or transferred to a securitization trust for
elements.label: text

elements.text: which Freddie Mac is a guarantor of some or all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments owed to holders of the securities issued by
elements.label: text

elements.text: that trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-Owned Mortgage Any Mortgage purchased by Freddie Mac that is owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac, in whole or in part, and which
elements.label: text

elements.text: currently is not owned by a securitization trust or
elements.label: text

elements.text: deposited into a pool backing participation certificates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: S
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Mortgage A Senior Subordinate Mortgage is a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: previously owned by Freddie Mac that Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: has transferred to a Senior Subordinate Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust A securitization trust formed by Freddie Mac into
elements.label: text

elements.text: which Freddie Mac transfers ownership of certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-Owned Mortgages, which become Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgages upon transfer; Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may serve as trustee, master servicer, master custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: and, with respect to certain securities issued by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust, guarantor of payments owed on those securities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers of Senior Subordinate Mortgages must follow specific guidelines to ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with Freddie Mac’s Servicing requirements. The following key points outline
elements.label: text

elements.text: these obligations:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicers of Senior Subordinate Mortgages must comply with all Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Guide, including the additional Servicing requirements specified in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: that are applicable only to Senior Subordinate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will provide prior written notice to the Servicer of the date on which a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-Owned Mortgage becomes a Senior Subordinate Mortgage subject to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements set forth in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In any Guide section that requires a Servicer to act in Freddie Mac’s best interests,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers of a Senior Subordinate Mortgage must continue to do so on behalf of Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac in its capacity as trustee and/or master servicer of a Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Except as set forth in Section 8601.4(a), unless a Servicer receives notification from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac that it services a Senior Subordinate Mortgage, the Servicer must not apply
elements.label: text

elements.text: any of the requirements set forth in this chapter but rather should continue Servicing in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with all other Servicing requirements of the Guide and applicable Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If any Servicing requirement in this Chapter 8601 is inconsistent with a Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirement set forth elsewhere in the Guide, the Servicer must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirement set forth in this Chapter 8601 with respect to any Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage or Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Freddie Mac’s rights and Servicer rights, duties and obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac retains all rights and remedies set forth in the Guide, including, but not limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: to, Section 8101.1(d), except as modified below with respect to the rights set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise specified in this chapter, a Servicer of a Senior Subordinate Mortgage shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue to have the same rights, duties and obligations with respect to Freddie Mac as it
elements.label: text

elements.text: does as a Servicer of any other Freddie Mac-Owned or Guaranteed Mortgage. Consistent
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Section 8101.1(c), the Servicer shall remain an independent contractor of Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may enter into a contract with a Senior Subordinate Trust provided it first
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtains written approval from Freddie Mac. Except as specified in a contract between the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer and Senior Subordinate Trust, a Servicer of Senior Subordinate Mortgages will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: owe any duties or obligations to a Senior Subordinate Trust nor will it have any rights against
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Senior Subordinate Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consistent with Section 9204.1(c), Freddie Mac’s approval or settlement of a workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not limit its right to review the Mortgage file and invoke its remedies under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide. If Freddie Mac determines as a result of its review of the Mortgage file or
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise discovers any failure by a Servicer to comply with Servicing requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Guide or any other Purchase Documents, Freddie Mac has the right to require the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Compensate Freddie Mac and hold it harmless for any loss, damage or expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including court costs, attorney fees and incentive payments) incurred by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Repurchase a Mortgage that Freddie Mac repurchased from a Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust at any time under any of the circumstances outlined in Section 3602.2(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Unauthorized Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to the rights set forth in Section 7101.1(b)(iv) regarding a Servicer’s transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: of its Servicing portfolio (in whole or in part) without Freddie Mac’s prior written
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval will result in the assessment of a compensatory fee as determined by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac not to exceed 1% of the Senior Subordinate Trust’s share of the UPB of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages that are being transferred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Servicer use of Servicing Tools
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any license, user agreement, term of use or similar document or requirement set forth in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide or other Purchase Document applicable to a Servicer’s electronic communications
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Freddie Mac or the Servicer’s use of any Servicing Tools in connection with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of Mortgages under the Guide shall govern, and the same terms, conditions, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations and requirements shall apply to, a Servicer’s electronic communications or use of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any Servicing Tools in connection with the Servicing of any Senior Subordinate Mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO owned by a Senior Subordinate Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the license, user agreement, terms of use or similar document in the Purchase Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not expressly permit the Servicer to use such Servicing Tools in connection with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of any Senior Subordinate Mortgage or REO owned by a Senior Subordinate Trust,
elements.label: text

elements.text: such use is hereby deemed permitted but subject to and conditioned on all other terms,
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions, obligations and limitations applicable to the Servicer’s use of the such Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tools contained in the applicable license, user agreement, terms of use or similar document
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Guide or applicable Purchase Document (including, but not limited to, all “as is”
elements.label: text

elements.text: language, all liability limitations and any restrictions governing use by third parties).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Borrower inquiries
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 8101.4(a) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must respond promptly to any Borrower inquiries about the ownership or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignee of a Senior Subordinate Mortgage. When responding to such inquiries, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may, after verifying the Borrower’s identity, inform the Borrower that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is owned by the Senior Subordinate Trust designated in the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: notice informing the Servicer that the Mortgage became a Senior Subordinate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac is master servicer and trustee to the Senior Subordinate Trust; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer services the Mortgage in accordance with Freddie Mac’s Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, as required by the Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is unable, for instance in a telephone conversation, to verify that it is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower who is requesting the information, the Borrower must request the information in
elements.label: text

elements.text: writing. If requested, in addition to the information in the preceding bullets, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must also provide the Borrower with Freddie Mac’s address, Freddie Mac’s telephone
elements.label: text

elements.text: number and the Freddie Mac loan number associated with the Borrower’s Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The address and telephone number that the Servicer must provide to the Borrower are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: [Insert legal name of Senior Subordinate Trust]
elements.label: text

elements.text: c/o FREDDIE MAC, Trustee
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8200 JONES BRANCH DRIVE
elements.label: text

elements.text: MCLEAN, VA 22102
elements.label: text

elements.text: (800) FREDDIE
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer provides the Borrower with the requested information by telephone, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must provide the same information to the Borrower in writing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Sections 7101.6(b), 7101.7(a), 7101.7(c) and 8101.4(d) for additional Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations related to Borrower inquiries.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8601.2: Limited power of attorney (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The brown font text used throughout this chapter denotes the special requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages. Text in black or green font denotes general
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements that also apply elsewhere in the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Limited power of attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requesting a limited power of attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Limited power of attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Senior Subordinate Trust appoints Freddie Mac as attorney-in-fact to act on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Senior Subordinate Trust for purposes of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Signing all documents necessary to service the Senior Subordinate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pursuing foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Releasing the lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Endorsing the Note and executing other instruments and documents in connection with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Senior Subordinate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Taking title to the Mortgaged Premises as a result of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure or
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Selling the Senior Subordinate Trust’s REO and taking all other necessary actions in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with the Servicing of a Senior Subordinate Mortgage and for the preservation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance and disposition of REO owned by the Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: Further, each Senior Subordinate Trust authorizes Freddie Mac to further delegate to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers Freddie Mac’s authority to act as attorney-in-fact on behalf of each Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Trust for the purposes stated above and, in furtherance of such authority, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac has provided the Servicer with a limited power of attorney to act as attorney-in-fact on
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of each Senior Subordinate Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before exercising the authority delegated by Freddie Mac under the limited power of
elements.label: text

elements.text: attorney, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determine, in consultation with its retained counsel, whether the Servicer may, under
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law, rely on the limited power of attorney before pursuing foreclosure on
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of the Senior Subordinate Trust or otherwise exercising the authority set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the limited power of attorney in connection with the Servicer’s Servicing of a Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage and/or taking action in connection with the preservation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: management or disposition of the REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer determines that it is unable to rely on the limited power of attorney in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with its intended exercise of any of the authority granted under the limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: power of attorney in connection with its obligations under the Guide, then the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) and await further instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Requesting a limited power of attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer that has determined they are in need of one or more limited powers of attorney in
elements.label: text

elements.text: order to properly service one or more Senior Subordinate Mortgages must submit a request to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) that includes the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The name of the applicable Senior Subordinate Trust, including the associated Series
elements.label: text

elements.text: number
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The legal name of the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A statement, in the subject line of the request, that specifies the request is for a limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: power of attorney on behalf of a Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The number of limited powers of attorney requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. A shipping label, included as an attachment, listing the name, mailing address and phone
elements.label: text

elements.text: number of the Servicer employee that will receive the limited powers of attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8601.3: Regulatory reporting and Internal Revenue
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Service compliance (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The brown font text used throughout this chapter denotes the special requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages. Text in black or green font denotes general
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements that also apply elsewhere in the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Form 1099-A, Acquisition or Abandonment of Secured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ IRS Form 1099-C, Cancellation of Debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ IRS Form 1099-MISC, Miscellaneous Income
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) IRS Form 1099-A
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 8106.2(b) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide IRS Form 1099-A to the IRS and the Borrower as required under
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 6050J of the Internal Revenue Code.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This reporting must be done whenever a Senior Subordinate Trust or a third party acquires an
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest in a property in full or partial satisfaction of the Senior Subordinate Trust’s secured
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt or when Freddie Mac or the Servicer knows or has reason to know that a property has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been abandoned. For the purposes of filing these reports, the following definitions apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Senior Subordinate Trust acquires an interest in Mortgaged Premises either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ On the date of the foreclosure sale or the date the Borrower’s right of redemption, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: any, expires, whichever occurs later, and the Senior Subordinate Trust or the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Senior Subordinate Trust’s behalf is the winning bidder for the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ At the time a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure is recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A third party acquires an interest at the time of the foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Abandonment has occurred when Freddie Mac or the Servicer has reason to know from
elements.label: text

elements.text: all the facts and circumstances concerning the status of the Mortgaged Premises that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower intended to and has permanently discarded the property from use. If a Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines that an abandonment has occurred and expects to commence foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceedings within three months, the reporting obligation generally arises at the end of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the three-month period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following events trigger the reporting requirement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Acquisition by the Senior Subordinate Trust (the Senior Subordinate Trust acquires the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises at a foreclosure sale or by deed-in-lieu of foreclosure)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Third-party sale (a third-party acquires the Mortgaged Premises at a foreclosure sale)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HUD, RHS or VA acquisition (the Mortgaged Premises were acquired by HUD, RHS or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the VA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Abandonment (the Mortgaged Premises have been abandoned, three months have passed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and foreclosure proceedings have not begun)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A completed IRS Form 1099-A must be filed electronically with the IRS on or before March
elements.label: text

elements.text: 31 of the year following the calendar year in which the reportable event occurred. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must also furnish the Borrower with an information statement on or before January
elements.label: text

elements.text: 31 of that year. The requirement for furnishing such statement to the Borrower can be
elements.label: text

elements.text: satisfied by sending a completed IRS Form 1099-A to the Borrower’s last known address.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The form must name the Senior Subordinate Trust that owns the Mortgage and identify it as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the owner of the Mortgage, name Freddie Mac trustee to that Senior Subordinate Trust, list
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s address and include a statement that the information is being reported to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS. On the form, the “account number” should include the nine-digit Freddie Mac loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: number, followed by one space and the six-digit Servicer number.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 8601.3(b) in the event that both IRS Form 1099-A and IRS Form 1099-C
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be filed as the result of a cancelation of debt in connection with a foreclosure or similar
elements.label: text

elements.text: action in the same year for the same Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instructions for completing IRS Form 1099-A are set forth in Form 1065A, Report of IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1099-A and Form 1099-C Filing for a Senior Subordinate Trust. Servicers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the IRS’s and the various States’ requirements, as amended from time to time,
elements.label: text

elements.text: for filing IRS Form 1099-A. Servicers should consult with either their tax advisors or the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: concerning questions on such requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Servicer must file all IRS Forms 1099-A with the IRS electronically.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Electronic reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must file their reports with the IRS no later than March 31 of the year following
elements.label: text

elements.text: the calendar year in which the reportable event occurred. Even though a Servicer reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the IRS electronically, the Servicer must provide a copy of IRS Form 1099-A to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower (copy B) and to those States that require it (copy C). Copy B must be furnished
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Borrower on or before January 31 of the year following the reportable event.
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS requirements for filing electronically are set forth in IRS Publication 1220,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Specifications for Electronic Filing of Forms 1097, 1098, 1099, 3921, 3922, 5498 and W￾2G. A Servicer may obtain this publication by downloading it from the IRS website at
elements.label: text

elements.text: www.irs.gov or by calling the IRS at 1-800-TAX-FORM (1-800-829-3676).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When filing electronically, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insert appropriate header information on the electronic report it files with the IRS in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the following record description:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appropriate header information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Field name Data description
elements.label: text

elements.text: “A” Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer’s TIN [Insert Senior Subordinate Trust’s TIN as provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the written notice from Freddie Mac]
elements.label: text

elements.text: First payer name line [Insert name of Senior Subordinate Trust as
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in the written notice from Freddie Mac]
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer shipping address c/o Freddie Mac, Trustee for [insert name of Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Trust]
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer shipping address 8200 Jones Branch Drive
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer city, State and ZIP
elements.label: text

elements.text: CodeTM
elements.label: text

elements.text: McLean, VA 22102-3100
elements.label: text

elements.text: “B” Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer’s account number The nine-digit Freddie Mac loan number and the six￾digit Seller/Servicer number, separating these two
elements.label: text

elements.text: numbers by one space
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify Freddie Mac that the Servicer reported to the IRS electronically:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ When the report is sent to the IRS, the Servicer must submit Form 1065A to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (see Directory 3)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Servicer should not send Freddie Mac copies of the report that it filed with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Correcting or voiding previously submitted IRS Forms 1099-A
elements.label: text

elements.text: To correct or void a previously submitted IRS Form 1099-A, the Servicer must refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS requirements to determine how to report either for electronic corrections or voids. To
elements.label: text

elements.text: avoid or minimize penalties that may be imposed by the IRS, the Servicer must report
elements.label: text

elements.text: corrections or voids to the IRS when an error is discovered. When corrections or voids
elements.label: text

elements.text: are submitted to the IRS, a copy of Form 1065A must be submitted to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 3). Form 1065A should indicate the number of corrected or voided IRS Forms
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1099-A submitted to the IRS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Filing accuracy and documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are responsible for completing IRS Form 1099-A and providing the information
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the IRS and to the Borrower in a timely and accurate manner. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain copies of all statements and reports that Freddie Mac requires the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide directly to the Borrowers and the IRS in compliance with Section 6050J of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Internal Revenue Code. The Servicer must make such copies available for examination
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac upon request until Freddie Mac agrees, in writing, that such records may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be destroyed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the IRS penalizes Freddie Mac or any Senior Subordinate Trust or assesses any fee for
elements.label: text

elements.text: failure to produce such information or because a Servicer failed to file a return or
elements.label: text

elements.text: statement or filed an untimely, incorrect or incomplete return or statement, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be required to reimburse Freddie Mac for all costs incurred by Freddie Mac or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust as a result of such penalty or assessment and an amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: representing Freddie Mac’s or any Senior Subordinate Trust’s total tax liability resulting
elements.label: text

elements.text: from such reimbursement. Such reimbursement will not be required if the Servicer can
elements.label: text

elements.text: show that it met the filing requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) IRS Form 1099-C
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 8106.2(c) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report cancelations of Borrowers’ Mortgage debt on IRS Form 1099-C, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: required under Section 6050P of the Internal Revenue Code for all cancelations of mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt of $600 or more occurring on or after January 1, 2005, with respect to any Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage. IRS Form 1099-C must be filed regardless of whether the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: must report the cancelation of debt as income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1065A includes instructions for completing IRS Form 1099-C.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Coordination with IRS Form 1099-A
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, in the same calendar year, the Servicer cancels a debt in connection with a foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: or abandonment of the Mortgaged Premises, it is not necessary to file both IRS Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1099-A (see Section 8601.3(a)) and IRS Form 1099-C for the same Borrower. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer will meet the filing requirement for IRS Form 1099-A by completing boxes 4, 5
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 7 on IRS Form 1099-C. However, the Servicer may complete both IRS Forms 1099-
elements.label: text

elements.text: A and 1099-C separately; in that case, do not complete boxes 4, 5 and 7 on IRS Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1099-C. (See Form 1065A for filing instructions for IRS Forms 1099-A and 1099-C.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Requesting taxpayer identification numbers (TINs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must make a reasonable effort to obtain the correct name and TIN of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower whose debt was canceled. If the Servicer does not obtain the TIN before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt is canceled, it must request the Borrower’s TIN. Such request must clearly notify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower that the IRS requires the Borrower to furnish the TIN and that failure to furnish
elements.label: text

elements.text: such TIN subjects the Borrower to a $50 penalty imposed by the IRS. Use IRS Form W￾9, Request for Taxpayer Identification Number and Certification, to request the TIN.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, a Borrower is not required to certify the TIN under penalties of perjury.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Exceptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is not required to report the following on IRS Form 1099-C:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Certain bankruptcies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Debt canceled in bankruptcy is not reported unless the debt was incurred for business
elements.label: text

elements.text: or investment purposes. Senior Subordinate Mortgages may be incurred either for
elements.label: text

elements.text: personal purposes or for business or investment purposes. Thus, Servicers should
elements.label: text

elements.text: only file IRS Form 1099-C for discharges of debt in bankruptcy if they are aware that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower is holding the property for investment and not as a Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: or second home, such as in the case of an Investment Property Mortgage, determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: at origination. In that case, report debt canceled for the later of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The year in which the amount of canceled debt first can be determined, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The year in which the debt is canceled in bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers do not need to include interest as part of the canceled debt in box 2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if interest is reported as part of the canceled debt in box 2, show the interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: separately in box 3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Nonprincipal amounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Nonprincipal amounts include penalties, fines, fees and administrative costs. These
elements.label: text

elements.text: do not need to be reported.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Release of a Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS Form 1099-C does not need to be filed if one of the Borrowers on a Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage is released as long as the remaining Borrowers remain liable
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the full amount of the unpaid Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Guarantor or surety.
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS Form 1099-C does not need to be filed for a guarantor or surety. A guarantor is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not a debtor for purposes of IRS Form 1099-C, even if demand for payment is made
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the guarantor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Multiple Borrowers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Senior Subordinate Mortgages originated after 1994 that involve Borrowers who
elements.label: text

elements.text: are jointly and severally liable for the Mortgage, report the entire amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: canceled debt on each Borrower’s IRS Form 1099-C. Multiple Borrowers are jointly
elements.label: text

elements.text: and severally liable for a debt if there is no clear and convincing evidence to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contrary. If it can be shown that joint and several liability does not exist, an IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1099-C is required for each Borrower for whom the Servicer canceled a debt of
elements.label: text

elements.text: $600 or more.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Senior Subordinate Mortgages originated before 1995, the Servicer must file IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1099-C only for the primary (or first-named) Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer knows or has reason to know that the multiple Borrowers were
elements.label: text

elements.text: husband and wife who were living at the same address when the debt was incurred,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and it has no information that these circumstances have changed, the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: file only one IRS Form 1099-C.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See the instructions to Form 1065A for the application of these rules to entity
elements.label: text

elements.text: borrowers (e.g., estates or trusts).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of these reports, the following definitions apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A debt may include all amounts owed, including stated principal, stated interest, fees,
elements.label: text

elements.text: penalties, administrative costs and fines. However, only stated principal is required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be reported. If accrued interest is included in the amount of the stated debt (in box 2),
elements.label: text

elements.text: then it must be reported in box 3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A debt is canceled on the date an identifiable event occurs. An identifiable event is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A discharge in bankruptcy under Title 11 of the U.S. Code (but see exceptions in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8601.3(b)(iii), above)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment making the debt unenforceable in a receivership,
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure or similar proceeding
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment when the statute of limitations for collecting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt expires or when the statutory period for filing a claim or beginning a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deficiency judgment proceeding expires. Expiration of the statute of limitations is
elements.label: text

elements.text: an identifiable event only when the Borrower’s affirmative statute of limitations
elements.label: text

elements.text: defense is upheld in a final judgment or decision of a court and the appeal period
elements.label: text

elements.text: has expired
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment when deficiency rights were not preserved
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the foreclosure process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment when Freddie Mac (or its vendor per Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9601.1(a)) makes the determination not to pursue a deficiency action post￾foreclosure and notifies the Servicer of such determination
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment when a creditor elects foreclosure remedies that
elements.label: text

elements.text: by law end or bar the creditor’s right to collect the debt. This event applies if
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection is barred by local law after a “power of sale” in the Mortgage or deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: of trust is exercised.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A cancelation or extinguishment due to a probate or similar proceeding
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A discharge of indebtedness under an agreement with the debtor to cancel the
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt at less than full consideration (e.g., a short sale). Freddie Mac will advise the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer if such an agreement is reached with a Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A discharge of indebtedness because of a decision or a defined policy of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: creditor to discontinue collection activity and cancel the debt. A creditor’s defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy can be in writing or an established business practice. A practice to stop
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection activity and abandon a debt when a particular nonpayment period
elements.label: text

elements.text: expires is a defined policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Facts and circumstances indicating that a debt was not canceled include the existence of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: lien relating to the debt (up to the value of the security) or the sale or packaging for sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the debt by the creditor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of a foreclosure sale where deficiency rights were preserved, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not initially file IRS Form 1099-C. Freddie Mac will determine whether to pursue
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection of the deficiency of that Mortgage. If Freddie Mac makes a determination not
elements.label: text

elements.text: to pursue collection of the deficiency, Freddie Mac will notify the Servicer in the report
elements.label: text

elements.text: described below, and the Servicer must then file IRS Form 1099-C.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must review the 1099-C Loan Detail report, accessible via the “Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting” tile of the Servicer’s Servicer Performance Profile (SPP) (see Exhibit 88,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Tools), by the end of January annually and ensure that IRS Form 1099-C is
elements.label: text

elements.text: filed with the IRS as required in Section 8601.3(b)(v) and is provided to the Borrower as
elements.label: text

elements.text: required in Section 8601.3(b)(vi) for all Senior Subordinate Mortgages in which the debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been discharged in the prior year. This report will list all Freddie Mac-Owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guaranteed Mortgages whereby Freddie Mac has determined not to pursue collection of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the deficiency on behalf of the Senior Subordinate Trust in the prior year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To help facilitate this annual review, from February 1 to December 31 each year,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers can monitor the 1099-C Loan Detail report in the SPP, which also provides a
elements.label: text

elements.text: tentative aggregate list of Mortgages for which Freddie Mac has decided to not pursue
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection of the deficiency on behalf of the Senior Subordinate Trust for the current year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may use this current year’s list to prepare for the required annual review and
elements.label: text

elements.text: any eventual IRS Form 1099-C filings; however, as the status of the Mortgage and/or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: cancelation of debt may subsequently change, any Servicer that chooses to use this
elements.label: text

elements.text: current year’s list in such a manner must, as part of the required annual review, reconcile
elements.label: text

elements.text: the final report against any IRS Form 1099-C filings already prepared.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Reporting IRS Form 1099-C to IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must file IRS Forms 1099-C on the Senior Subordinate Trust’s behalf. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must file all IRS Forms 1099-C with the IRS electronically.
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS Publication 1220, Specifications for Electronic Filing of Forms 1097, 1098, 1099,
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3921, 3922, 5498 and W-2G, sets forth the IRS requirements for filing electronic reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: and corrections. The Servicer can obtain this publication by downloading it from the IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: website at http://www.irs.gov/, or by calling 1-800-TAX-FORM (1-800-829-3676).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When filing electronically through the IRS Filing Information Returns Electronically
elements.label: text

elements.text: (FIRE) System, the Servicer must insert appropriate header information on the report it
elements.label: text

elements.text: files with the IRS in accordance with the following record descriptions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS FIRE System header information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Field name Data description
elements.label: text

elements.text: “A” Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer’s TIN [Insert Senior Subordinate Trust’s TIN as
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in the written notice from Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac]
elements.label: text

elements.text: First payer name line [Insert name of Senior Subordinate Trust as
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in the written notice from Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac]
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer shipping address c/o Freddie Mac, Trustee for [insert name of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust]
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer shipping address 8200 Jones Branch Drive
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer city, State and ZIP CodeTM McLean, VA 22102-3100
elements.label: text

elements.text: “B” Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payer’s account number The nine-digit Freddie Mac loan number and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the six-digit Seller/Servicer number,
elements.label: text

elements.text: separating these two numbers by one space
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Servicer must file its reports with the IRS not later than March 31 of the year
elements.label: text

elements.text: following the calendar year in which the cancelation of debt occurs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Reporting the IRS Form 1099-C to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Even though a Servicer reports to the IRS electronically, the Servicer is still responsible
elements.label: text

elements.text: for providing a paper copy of IRS Form 1099-C to the Borrower (copy B) by January 31
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the year following the calendar year in which the cancelation of debt occurred. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer can satisfy the requirement for furnishing such statement to the Borrower by
elements.label: text

elements.text: sending completed IRS Form 1099-C to the Borrower’s last known address. The form
elements.label: text

elements.text: must identify the Senior Subordinate Trust that owns the Senior Subordinate Mortgage,
elements.label: text

elements.text: name Freddie Mac as Trustee to the Senior Subordinate Trust, list Freddie Mac’s address
elements.label: text

elements.text: and include a statement that the information is being reported to the IRS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is also required to file IRS Form 1099-C with any State that requires this
elements.label: text

elements.text: filing in accordance with the State’s filing deadlines.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii) Notification to Freddie Mac of electronic reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac that the Servicer reported IRS Form 1099-C to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS. When the electronic report is sent to the IRS, the Servicer must submit Form 1065A
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the attention of (see Directory 3).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) Correcting or voiding previously submitted IRS Form 1099-C
elements.label: text

elements.text: To correct or void a previously submitted IRS Form 1099-C, the Servicer must refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: IRS requirements to determine how to report either electronic corrections or voids. To
elements.label: text

elements.text: avoid or minimize penalties that may be imposed by the IRS, the Servicer must promptly
elements.label: text

elements.text: report corrections or voids to the IRS when an error is discovered. When the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: submits corrections or voids to the IRS, the Servicer must also submit a copy of Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1065A to Freddie Mac (see Directory 3). The Servicer must indicate on Form 1065A the
elements.label: text

elements.text: number of corrected or voided IRS Forms 1099-C submitted to the IRS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ix) Filing accuracy and documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are responsible for completing IRS Form 1099-C and for providing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information to the IRS and to the Borrower in a timely and accurate manner. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain copies of all statements and reports that Freddie Mac requires the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: to provide directly to the Borrowers and the IRS in compliance with Section 6050P of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Internal Revenue Code
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Make such copies available for examination by Freddie Mac upon request until
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac agrees, in writing, that such records may be destroyed (which will be a
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum of four years)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the IRS penalizes Freddie Mac or the Senior Subordinate Trust or assesses any fees for
elements.label: text

elements.text: failure to produce such information, or because the Servicer failed to file a return or
elements.label: text

elements.text: statement or filed an untimely, incorrect or incomplete return or statement, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be required to reimburse Freddie Mac for all costs incurred by Freddie Mac or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust as a result of such penalty or assessment and for the amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: representing Freddie Mac’s or any Senior Subordinate Trust’s total tax liability resulting
elements.label: text

elements.text: from such reimbursement. Freddie Mac will not require such reimbursement if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer can show that it met the filing requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) IRS Form 1099-MISC
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 8106.2(d) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should not prepare or file IRS Form 1099-MISC using either Freddie Mac’s or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust’s names or Taxpayer Identification Numbers. The Servicer should
elements.label: text

elements.text: consult with its tax advisor to review its reporting obligations with regard to the filing of IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1099-MISC.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8601.4 Transfer of Servicing, loan assumptions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower notices and required documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The brown font text used throughout this chapter denotes the special requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages. Text in black or green font denotes general
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements that also apply elsewhere in the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Concurrent and Subsequent Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Declination of the application for assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Modifying model letters for Senior Subordinate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Concurrent and Subsequent Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements governing Concurrent Transfers of Servicing and Subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfers of Servicing set forth elsewhere in the Guide, Servicer must inform Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Transferee Servicer of any Transfers of Servicing with respect to Mortgages that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: being sold or were sold to Freddie Mac under any Senior Subordinate Mortgage Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document by designating on Form 960, Agreement for Concurrent Transfer of Servicing of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Single-Family Mortgages, or in the Electronic Agreement (as defined in Section 1401.1(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Subsequent Transfer of Servicing of Single-Family Mortgages (“STOS Agreement”) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Freddie Mac Servicing Transfer Manager (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools), as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, whether the Transfer of Servicing involves such Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the Seller/Transferor Servicer must, in the list of Mortgages that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Transferor Servicer provides to the Transferee Servicer and Freddie Mac, indicate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following status of each such Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage has not yet been transferred to a Senior Subordinate Trust:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether the Mortgage is subject to a Senior Subordinate Mortgage Purchase Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether the Mortgage is the subject of a notice from Freddie Mac under Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8601.1(b) that designates the date on which the Mortgage will become a Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage has been transferred to a Senior Subordinate Trust:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether the Mortgage is subject to a Senior Subordinate Mortgage Purchase Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The name of the Senior Subordinate Trust that holds the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to each Mortgage transferred to a Senior Subordinate Trust and subject to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Servicing, the Seller/Transferor Servicer must promptly provide the Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage Purchase Document and any notice issued by Freddie Mac under
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8601.1(b) to the Transferee Servicer. The Seller/Transferor Servicer also must
elements.label: text

elements.text: inform the Transferee Servicer as to the status of any post-funding quality control review that
elements.label: text

elements.text: is underway for Mortgages that have not yet been transferred to a Senior Subordinate Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferee Servicer must comply with any obligations set forth in the Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage Purchase Document including, but not limited to, specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements pertaining to post-funding quality control reviews and Tri-Party Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: version requirements (e.g., using a version of Form 1035, Document Custodial Agreement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Single-Family Mortgages; Form 1035DC, Designated Custodial Agreement: Single-Family
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages (The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A.); or Form 1035CS,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Designated Custodial Agreement: Single-Family Mortgages, revised on or after February
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of Bulletin 2015-5, a Transferee Servicer’s execution of Form 960 or the STOS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement constitutes an agreement by the Transferee Servicer with Freddie Mac to service
elements.label: text

elements.text: the transferred Mortgages that the Transferor Servicer designated as those that are or may
elements.label: text

elements.text: become Senior Subordinate Mortgages in accordance with the requirements of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8601.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Declination of the application for assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 8406.2(d) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the application for a Mortgage assumption is declined, the Servicer must provide an
elements.label: text

elements.text: adverse action notice to all applicable parties or any other notice or disclosure required under
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Fair Credit Reporting Act, Truth in Lending Act and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other applicable federal, State or local law. Neither Freddie Mac nor the Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust may be identified as a creditor in any notice or disclosure that may be required, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: neither Freddie Mac nor the Senior Subordinate Trust is participating in the decision on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Modifying model letters for Senior Subordinate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: When denying a Borrower for a loan modification of a Senior Subordinate Mortgage based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the First Complete Borrower Response Package, the Servicer must amend the appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 93, Evaluation Notices, model clause language pertaining to the Mortgage Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regulatory Notice and Right of Appeal by adding the following sentence after the phrase
elements.label: text

elements.text: “You were not approved for a loan modification Trial Period Plan”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Your Mortgage is owned by [name of Senior Subordinate Trust],
elements.label: text

elements.text: which requires your loan to be serviced in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: servicing requirements of Freddie Mac, the Master Servicer of your
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: and deleting the phrase “the owner of your Mortgage loan” after “Freddie Mac” in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: sentence that currently reads:
elements.label: text

elements.text: You were evaluated for mortgage payment assistance based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the eligibility requirements of Freddie Mac, the owner of your
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers have the option to use the model clauses provided in Exhibit 93 or develop
elements.label: text

elements.text: customized equivalent model clauses to meet the requirement. However, before using any
elements.label: text

elements.text: model clauses provided in Exhibit 93 or a customized equivalent, Servicers must ensure they
elements.label: text

elements.text: are modified, where applicable, to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify the appropriate Senior Subordinate Trust as the owner of a Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, instead of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify Freddie Mac as the master servicer, rather than the owner of the Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Inform the Borrower that the Senior Subordinate Mortgage is serviced in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s Servicing requirements, as obligated by the Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Clarify that any eligibility decisions were made in accordance with Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8601.5: Default management, foreclosure processes
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: and legal considerations (12/03/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The brown font text used throughout this chapter denotes the special requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages. Text in black or green font denotes general
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements that also apply elsewhere in the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements for Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referral to foreclosure documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclosing in the Servicer’s name
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expedited foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deficiency rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-routine default-related legal matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Counsel retained by Servicers pursuant to Servicer’s duty to indemnify Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retention of firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referral of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General requirements for Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9301.1(b) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When following the requirements provided in Chapter 9301 while processing Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default Legal Matters, the Servicer must comply with:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The terms and conditions of the Mortgage documents, including the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Applicable federal, State and local laws and customs
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Requirements of the FHA, VA, RHS or MI, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Guide and other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The limited power of attorney granted to the Servicer by Freddie Mac on behalf of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Referral to foreclosure documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9301.2(f) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Senior Subordinate Mortgage, appropriate foreclosure documentation includes, but is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to, all documents required by the foreclosure counsel to complete the first legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: action. The Servicer must continue to comply with applicable law but at a minimum must
elements.label: text

elements.text: supply the foreclosure counsel with the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A statement that informs foreclosure counsel that the Senior Subordinate Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: owned by a Senior Subordinate Trust and that also provides counsel with the name of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A copy of the limited power of attorney provided to the Servicer by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorizing the Servicer to pursue foreclosure on behalf of the Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Instructions, given along with the limited power of attorney, requiring foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: counsel to inform the Servicer if counsel determines upon referral or at any later time that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the limited power of attorney provided to the Servicer by Freddie Mac cannot be relied
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon under applicable law*
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Copies of the Note (or the original Note if required by applicable law) evidencing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: indebtedness along with any intervening assignments, endorsements or any applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: modifying instrument, such as a modification agreement, a conversion agreement or an
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption of indebtedness and release of liability agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Mortgage or deed of trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Copy of the original title insurance policy. For Cooperative Share Loans recognized as
elements.label: text

elements.text: personal property, refer to Section 8801.1(f) regarding certain Servicer warranties when
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosing on a Cooperative Share Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Copy of the breach, acceleration or demand letter sent to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Military affidavits
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. Executed Substitution of Trustee, as necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. Payoff statement with per diem interest as of the date of the foreclosure referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: 11. Send foreclosure counsel the following information by facsimile transmission or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: electronic means:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name, mailing address and telephone number of the Borrower(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property address (if different from the Borrower’s mailing address)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A statement that the Senior Subordinate Mortgage is a Freddie Mac Guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage and that includes the nine-digit Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name and address of the person to contact in the Servicer’s foreclosure department
elements.label: text

elements.text: *The Servicer must comply with any applicable requirements in Section 8601.1(d) if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer makes the determination, on advice of foreclosure counsel or otherwise, that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited power of attorney cannot be relied upon and the Servicer has also determined that a
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited power of attorney issued directly by the Senior Subordinate Trust and/or action by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, such as the execution of documents, is necessary to the proper Servicing of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Referral to foreclosure documentation requirements on a Senior Subordinate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured by a Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the above requirements, if the Mortgage is secured by a Manufactured Home,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must notify the foreclosure counsel that the property is a Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: when it submits the case to the foreclosure counsel. The Servicer must also provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure counsel with evidence that the property is legally classified as real property under
elements.label: text

elements.text: the laws in the State where the property is located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Evidence that the property is real property in a non-certificate of title State
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide the foreclosure counsel with copies of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation in non-certificate of title States (see Section 5703.5) evidencing that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home and the land are real property under the laws of the State where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property is located:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information stating the legal basis (statutory or common law) for determining that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home is real property that is exempt from certificate of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements. This information may be included in the documentation received at
elements.label: text

elements.text: origination of the Senior Subordinate Mortgage from the title insurance company that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Manufactured Home is real property, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence that a certificate of title has not been issued, such as the manufacturer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: statement of origin, if the manufacturer’s statement of origin is not required to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: surrendered to a State agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Evidence that the property is real property in a certificate of title surrender State
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide the foreclosure counsel with copies of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation in certificate of title surrender States (see Section 5703.5) evidencing that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Manufactured Home and the land are real property under the laws of the State where
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property is located:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A certificate of cancelation, notification letter or other acknowledgment from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: State motor vehicles administration or the appropriate State agency to which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: certificate of title was surrendered, or a copy of the documents submitted in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with the surrender along with evidence that the documents were delivered
elements.label: text

elements.text: and received by the appropriate State agency, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copies of the documents, such as an affidavit of affixture, recorded in the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: records as part of the title surrender procedures to show the Manufactured Home has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been converted to real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Evidence of clear and marketable title to the Manufactured Home and land in
elements.label: text

elements.text: certificate of title States
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide the foreclosure counsel with the following documentation in
elements.label: text

elements.text: certificate of title States (see Section 5703.5) evidencing the Borrower’s ownership of
elements.label: text

elements.text: both the Manufactured Home and the land on which it is permanently affixed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation evidencing that the land is legally classified as real property under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: laws in the State where the property is located:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The original or a copy of the certificate of title showing the Borrower as owner of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home. The certificate of title must have a notation of the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s security interest in the Manufactured Home in the name of the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: and its successors in interest and assigns and have a notation of all intervening
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignments from the original mortgagee to each successive Servicer, ending with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current Servicer, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the deed evidencing ownership of the land showing the owner of the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the deed to be identical to the owner of the Manufactured Home on the certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: This and any other relevant information must be provided within five days of the referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: to foreclosure so that the foreclosure counsel has the information necessary to
elements.label: text

elements.text: simultaneously enforce the liens (whenever possible) and so as not to unnecessarily
elements.label: text

elements.text: lengthen the foreclosure process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Foreclosing in the Servicer’s name
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9301.3(c) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Conducting the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must instruct the foreclosure counsel to process the foreclosure in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s name and in a manner that would avoid any obligation to pay a transfer tax.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, the Servicer may instruct foreclosure counsel to conduct the foreclosure in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust’s name if applicable law:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Precludes the Servicer from conducting the foreclosure in its name because it owns or
elements.label: text

elements.text: services a subordinate Mortgage on the Mortgaged Premises, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requires the foreclosure to be processed in the Senior Subordinate Trust’s name to
elements.label: text

elements.text: avoid any obligation to pay a transfer tax and foreclosure counsel could not otherwise
elements.label: text

elements.text: process the foreclosure in a manner that would successfully avoid imposition of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer tax obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: When Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages, the Servicer may not, under any
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstance, foreclose or take title in Freddie Mac’s name, even if doing so would be
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary to avoid any obligation to pay a transfer tax.
elements.label: text
