elements.text: For these special circumstances, the Servicer does not need to obtain written approval but
elements.label: text

elements.text: must notify Freddie Mac within two Business Days of the Servicer’s determination to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclose in the Senior Subordinate Trust’s name and record the basis of the decision in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage file. All notifications must be sent via e-mail (see Directory 5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all other circumstances in which the Servicer may need to instruct foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: counsel to conduct the foreclosure in the Senior Subordinate Trust’s name, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must obtain written approval from Freddie Mac (refer to Section 8601.5(f) regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: initiating legal actions on the Senior Subordinate Trust’s behalf).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When processing the foreclosure in the Senior Subordinate Trust’s name, all pleadings
elements.label: text

elements.text: and related documents must comply with Section 8601.5(f). The Servicer remains
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligated to notify Freddie Mac pursuant to Section 9501.5(d) in the event that any
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure conducted in the Senior Subordinate Trust’s name evolves into a non-routine
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation matter (see Section 8601.5(f)).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Servicer conducts the foreclosure in the Senior Subordinate Trust’s name, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is not permitted to have the same foreclosure counsel represent the Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: another lienholder in the same proceeding. Freddie Mac, as master servicer and/or trustee
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Senior Subordinate Trust, does not consent to dual representation of the Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Trust and another lienholder on the same property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Executing documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac needs to execute a document for the Servicer to process the foreclosure,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or execute a document related to a foreclosure sale, the Servicer must submit Form 105,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Multipurpose Loan Servicing Transmittal, to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: supporting documentation, which may include, but is not limited to, the last recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: document in the chain of title, and include the document Freddie Mac needs to execute.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If an assignment of the Security Instrument to the Senior Subordinate Trust has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded and the Servicer is conducting the foreclosure in the Servicer’s name, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument must be assigned back to the Servicer before the foreclosure counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: files the first legal action. Refer to Section 9301.5(a)(i) for an explanation of first legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: action.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The limited power of attorney provided to the Servicer by Freddie Mac authorizing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer to pursue foreclosure on behalf of the Senior Subordinate Trust should provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer with the authority to assign the Security Instrument assigned back to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer. The Servicer must comply with any applicable requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8601.2(a) if the Servicer makes the determination that the limited power of attorney
elements.label: text

elements.text: granted to the Servicer by Freddie Mac cannot be relied upon under the applicable State
elements.label: text

elements.text: or local law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is foreclosing on a Senior Subordinate Mortgage registered with MERS®,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must prepare and execute (using the Servicer’s employee who is a MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized “signing officer”) an assignment of the Security Instrument from MERS to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer. The Servicer must record the prepared assignment where required by State law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: State mandated recordings are non-reimbursable by Freddie Mac, are not considered part
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Freddie Mac allowable foreclosure counsel fees and must not be billed to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Senior Subordinate Mortgage is an FHA, Section 502 GRH or VA Mortgage, then
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must follow FHA, RHS or VA guidelines to determine in whose name the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure action should be brought.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Sections 6301.2(d) and 6301.4(a) for additional information on Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for assignments of the Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Expedited foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Sections 9301.5(b), 9301.5(d) and 9301.5(e) to
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflect special Servicing requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must always consider how to resolve a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: legally compliant manner that obtains the best result for the Senior Subordinate Trust. While
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are not required to use expedited methods identified in Chapter 9301 or
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding expedited methods in this chapter, if a Servicer does proceed with an
elements.label: text

elements.text: expedited method, it must adhere to the requirements set forth in those chapters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9301 and this section provide requirements for expediting Freddie Mac Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Matters, such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Waiving the Senior Subordinate Trust’s right to pursue deficiency actions against a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expediting the foreclosure if the Mortgaged Premises is identified as vacant/abandoned
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtaining default judgment for an amount less than total amount owed by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State-specific methods for expediting Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Expedited foreclosures – Illinois
elements.label: text

elements.text: In Illinois, Servicers may obtain a consent judgment from a Borrower and take title to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises in the name of the Senior Subordinate Trust instead of selling the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises at foreclosure sale. In doing so, Servicers must follow the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Section 9301.5(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Expedited foreclosures – New York
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may use the New York Foreclosure Inquest Program as a method to expedite
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure actions on Senior Subordinate Mortgages in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Section 9301.5(e).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Deficiency rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: In implementing the requirements of Sections 9301.6(a)(ii), 9301.6(b), 9601.1(a) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9601.1(b), all references concerning the right to pursue a deficiency are the right of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust with respect to Senior Subordinate Mortgages. In addition, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must ensure that any consent judgment in Illinois results in title to the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises being conveyed to the Senior Subordinate Trust and not to Freddie Mac or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Non-routine default-related legal matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9402.1(b) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to direct and control all litigation involving a Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage, regardless of whether Freddie Mac or the Senior Subordinate Trust is
elements.label: text

elements.text: a named party. The Servicer and any law firm handling the litigation must cooperate fully
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Freddie Mac in the prosecution, defense and handling of the matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Definition of routine and non-routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Routine litigation generally is a contested action in which the Borrower alleges case￾specific defenses or issues that, if successful, would not create negative legal precedent
elements.label: text

elements.text: beyond the immediate case.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-routine litigation generally is a contested action in which the Borrower alleges
elements.label: text

elements.text: case-specific defenses or issues that, if successful, would create negative legal precedent
elements.label: text

elements.text: beyond the immediate case.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of non-routine litigation that must be reported to Freddie Mac as non-routine
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation include, but are not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actions that name Freddie Mac and/or the Senior Subordinate Trust as a party
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Action that seeks monetary relief against Freddie Mac and/or the Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust, including any claim (including counterclaims, cross-claims or third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: claims in foreclosure or bankruptcy actions) for damages against Freddie Mac or its
elements.label: text

elements.text: officers, directors, or employees and/or the Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actions that challenge the validity, priority or enforceability of a Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or seek to impair Freddie Mac’s and/or the Senior Subordinate Trust’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest in an REO, including, by way of example:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. An action seeking to demolish a structure on the property or the property as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: result of a code violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. An action seeking to avoid a lien based on a failure to comply with a law or
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. An attempt by a junior lienholder to assert priority over a Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or extinguish Freddie Mac’s or the Senior Subordinate Trust’s interests
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. A quiet title action seeking to declare the Senior Subordinate Trust’s lien void,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. An attempt by a Borrower to effect a cramdown of a Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage in bankruptcy as to which Freddie Mac has not delegated authority to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer or law firm to address
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actions that present an issue that may pose significant legal or reputational risk to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, including, by way of example:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Any issue involving Freddie Mac’s conservatorship, its conservator (FHFA),
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s status as a federal instrumentality or an interpretation of Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s charter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Any assertion that Freddie Mac is a federal agency or otherwise part of the U.S.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Government
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Any “due process” or other constitutional challenge
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Any challenge to the methods by which Freddie Mac does business
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Any putative class actions involving a Senior Subordinate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Challenges to the standing of the Servicer or the Senior Subordinate Trust to
elements.label: text

elements.text: conduct foreclosures or bankruptcies that, if successful, could create negative
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal precedent with an impact beyond the immediate case
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Challenges to the methods by which MERS does business or its ability to act as
elements.label: text

elements.text: nominee under a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Any “show cause orders” or motions for sanctions relating to a Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage, whether against a Senior Subordinate Trust, Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer, a law firm or a vendor of the Servicer or law firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. Any appellate or other action for post-judgment relief in any foreclosure,
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy or legal action in which Freddie Mac and/or the Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust is a named party
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. Foreclosures on HUD-Guaranteed Section 184 Native American Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 11. Any environmental litigation relating to a Senior Subordinate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12. A need to foreclose judicially in a State where non-judicial foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: predominate
elements.label: text

elements.text: 13. Any claim brought by a governmental body
elements.label: text

elements.text: 14. Cross-border insolvency proceedings under Chapter 15 of the Bankruptcy Code
elements.label: text

elements.text: 15. Any claim of predatory lending or discrimination in Mortgage origination or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 16. Any claim implicating the interpretation of the terms of the Fannie Mae/Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Uniform Mortgage Instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Given the evolving nature of default-related litigation, it is not possible to provide an
elements.label: text

elements.text: exhaustive list of non-routine litigation. Each contested action presents unique
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances, and the Servicer should evaluate each action on a case-by-case basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: to determine whether a contested action is routine or non-routine.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Legal actions and strategies initiated by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must obtain written approval (see Directory 5) from the Freddie Mac Legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Division prior to initiating the following legal actions and strategies:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filing a new legal action, other than a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter, on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and/or the Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filing a motion to intervene in a pending legal action on behalf of Freddie Mac and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appealing or otherwise challenging a judgment in any foreclosure or bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeding, or any legal action in which Freddie Mac and/or the Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust is a named party
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filing a notice of removal to federal district court for any legal action in which
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and/or the Senior Subordinate Trust is a named party
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Asserting any position in a legal action that relates to Freddie Mac’s status as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: government-sponsored enterprise, its conservatorship or FHFA
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Propounding discovery requests or otherwise serving or providing any discovery
elements.label: text

elements.text: responses on behalf of Freddie Mac and/or the Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Referring to Freddie Mac in litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac must be described in legal proceedings as “Federal Home Loan Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation (“Freddie Mac”), a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: United States of America.” Freddie Mac may not be referred to as a “government
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Referring to the Senior Subordinate Trust in litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Senior Subordinate Trust must be described in legal proceedings as “[insert legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: name], an entity organized and existing under the laws of the United States of America.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers and counsel must not refer to any Senior Subordinate Trust as a “government
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) MERS-registered Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 8101.5 for additional requirements relating to notices from MERS and
elements.label: text

elements.text: MERS-registered Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Counsel retained by Servicers pursuant to Servicer’s duty to indemnify Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9402.2(b) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: From time to time, the Servicer may retain counsel to represent Freddie Mac, the Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Trust and the Servicer, or any combination thereof, with respect to litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: involving, related to or arising out of allegations which, if true, could subject the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: liability to Freddie Mac for a failure to comply with any selling or Servicing representation
elements.label: text

elements.text: or warranty or requirement of the Guide or other Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer retains counsel for this purpose, the Servicer remains liable for legal fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: and costs incurred in the defense of any litigation, as well as any and all losses, judgments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: damages and expenses, including fees and costs entered against and incurred on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for the Senior Subordinate Trust’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: proportionate share of expenses for responding to Borrower defenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Counsel retained and paid by Servicers pursuant to Section 8601.5(f) and this section do not
elements.label: text

elements.text: need to be selected and engaged pursuant to Chapter 9501. In all other Freddie Mac Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Legal Matters, the Servicer must use counsel selected and engaged pursuant to Chapter 9501.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) of its retention of counsel not selected
elements.label: text

elements.text: and engaged pursuant to Chapter 9501 and are required to comply with the reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 9402.2(a)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that the law firm to which the Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter
elements.label: text

elements.text: was originally referred is updated on the current status of the litigation either by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: or its new counsel. Once the litigation for which new counsel is retained concludes, unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: the entire legal matter or action is resolved, the Servicer is responsible for transitioning the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter back to the law firm to which it was originally referred for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any required further proceedings no later than one Business Day after the resolution of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: litigation matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In instances in which the Security Instrument provides for the Borrower to reimburse any
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal fees and costs incurred by the Servicer, the Servicer should instruct its counsel to notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower about his or her responsibility for such expenses. The Servicer’s counsel should
elements.label: text

elements.text: attempt to handle such matters by stipulation or any other expeditious manner that will
elements.label: text

elements.text: reduce the fees and costs that the Borrower has to pay.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Retention of firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9501.3(b) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Servicer contract with firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless the Servicer has entered into a contract with a selected firm and Freddie Mac has
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided a “no objection” determination, then the Servicer must enter into a contract with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the firm. The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac when the contract has been executed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: updating the Servicer Attorney Tracking System (SATS) via
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://www.freddiemacsats.com and must provide a copy of the contract to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Freddie Mac limited retention agreement with firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, on its behalf and on behalf of each Senior Subordinate Trust that Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac creates, will enter into a limited retention agreement that sets forth certain key
elements.label: text

elements.text: retention provisions with each selected firm for each State in which the firm has received
elements.label: text

elements.text: a “no objection” determination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Conflict between Servicer’s contract and limited retention agreements; Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: respective consent
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer acknowledges that the limited retention agreement recognizes and reflects a
elements.label: text

elements.text: joint attorney-client relationship between the law firm, Freddie Mac, each Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Trust and the Servicer, and the Servicer consents to such joint representation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer consents, in advance, to the selected firm’s representation of Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Senior Subordinate Trust or both in any Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter that is or
elements.label: text

elements.text: might be adverse to the Servicer and further agrees that the firm can use in such
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation any information the firm gained in the course of jointly representing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer, Freddie Mac and the Senior Subordinate Trust. In the event of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: inconsistency or conflict between the terms and conditions of the Servicer’s contract with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the selected firm and the terms and conditions of Freddie Mac’s limited retention
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement with the firm, Freddie Mac’s limited retention agreement shall control.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Referral of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9501.4(b) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Requirements prior to referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to referring a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter to a firm, the Servicer must confirm
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the firm is eligible to receive a referral by ensuring that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm meets the Firm Minimum Requirements, as specified in Section 9501.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac has provided a “no objection” determination, as specified in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm has executed a contract with the Servicer requiring the firm to comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: all applicable Freddie Mac requirements, as specified in Section 8601.5(h)(ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm has executed a limited retention agreement with Freddie Mac, as specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 8601.5(h)(ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The firm has completed Freddie Mac training and any additional Servicer training, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in Section 9501.4(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are no conflicts of interest with respect to the retention of the firm and referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to the firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Diversification of referrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must diversify its referrals of Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate number of firms in each State to protect the interests of Freddie Mac and to
elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigate the risks related to a high concentration of Freddie Mac files. In selecting firms
elements.label: text

elements.text: for referrals, the Servicer must consider firm capacity and management of staff-to-file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratios.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Bankruptcy and foreclosure matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not refer foreclosure matters directly to trustees listed on deeds of
elements.label: text

elements.text: trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9401.3(b)(ii) for additional referral requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Providing documentation to firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must identify a file as a Freddie Mac Default Legal Matter when sending
elements.label: text

elements.text: the file to a firm. In this file, the Servicer must also include the name of the Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Trust that owns the delinquent Senior Subordinate Mortgage that prompted
elements.label: text

elements.text: the referral and identify the Senior Subordinate Trust as owner of the Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage and Freddie Mac as both master servicer and trustee to the Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When referring a file to a firm, the Servicer must provide all documentation required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: initiate a foreclosure. If the firm requests any additional information and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation at any time, the Servicer must provide such requested information and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents within three Business Days after receipt of the request or within such earlier
elements.label: text

elements.text: time frame if necessary to comply with timing requirements under applicable law or court
elements.label: text

elements.text: orders and procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any Mortgage that the Servicer refers for foreclosure that is subsequently repurchased
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac from the Senior Subordinate Trust and/or subsequently repurchased by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer from Freddie Mac, whether voluntarily or involuntarily, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify foreclosure and/or bankruptcy counsel within two Business Days of the completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase. (See Chapter 3602 for additional information about repurchases.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Contingency plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Servicers must have a contingency plan in place, either in the form of a standalone
elements.label: text

elements.text: document or incorporated into policies and procedures, to redirect new foreclosure and
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy referrals.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8601.6: Property preservation, title management and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: lien considerations (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The brown font text used throughout this chapter denotes the special requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages. Text in black or green font denotes general
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements that also apply elsewhere in the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preserving the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expenses that may become First Liens on the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery of clear and marketable title
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Vesting the title and avoiding transfer taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Preserving the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must comply with the requirements of Section 9301.6(d) except that, with
elements.label: text

elements.text: respect to Senior Subordinate Mortgages, either the Servicer or the Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: would be the mortgagee, not Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Expenses that may become First Liens on the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to the Servicing of Senior Subordinate Mortgages, the Servicer must protect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust’s interest in the property and its First Lien Position to the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: extent that it is obligated under Section 9301.6(e), with respect to Freddie Mac-Owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guaranteed Mortgages to protect “Freddie Mac’s interest in the property” and “Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s First Lien Position.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Delivery of clear and marketable title
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9301.10(b) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Property located in a State without a redemption or confirmation period
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer is the purchaser of the property at a foreclosure sale, it must ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the foreclosure counsel provides the Senior Subordinate Trust with clear and
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketable title to the property after the foreclosure sale. The title must be free of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: liens, claims, defects and encumbrances. The title must be marketable so Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: can sell the property freely to others on behalf of the Senior Subordinate Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must instruct the foreclosure counsel to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Submit the foreclosure deed for recordation within one Business Day after receipt of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Obtain the recorder’s receipt as evidence that the deed was presented for recordation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Send the Servicer the recorder’s receipt within three Business Days after receiving it
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the recorder
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Provide the recorded deed to the Servicer within three Business Days after receiving
elements.label: text

elements.text: the deed from the recorder’s office. The Servicer must retain the deed in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Property located in a State with a redemption or confirmation period
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the redemption period has expired or the foreclosure sale has been confirmed, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must ensure that clear and marketable title is obtained as stated in this Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Executing documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac or the Senior Subordinate Trust needs to execute a document for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer to process the foreclosure or execute a document related to a foreclosure sale,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must submit Form 105, Multipurpose Loan Servicing Transmittal, to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac (see Directory 5) with all supporting documentation, which may include, but is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, the last recorded document in the chain of title, and include the document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac or the Senior Subordinate Trust needs to execute.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Vesting the title and avoiding transfer taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9301.10(c) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the foreclosure sale, or when closing a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure transaction, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must ensure that title to the foreclosed property purchased by the Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust is vested to the appropriate party. (See Section 9209.6 regarding closing, reporting and
elements.label: text

elements.text: remittance requirements for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure transaction.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Conventional Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the foreclosure sale, or when closing a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure transaction, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must ensure that title to the foreclosed property is vested in the legal name of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Senior Subordinate Trust if the property is not purchased by a third party. The property
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not at any time vest in the Servicer’s or Freddie Mac’s name, even when doing so
elements.label: text

elements.text: would provide additional benefits to the Senior Subordinate Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for any transfer taxes unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Local authorities require the Servicer to pay the transfer tax to record a deed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that title vests appropriately
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The transfer tax is paid under protest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer submits the request for written pre-approval for reimbursement of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer tax via PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 88, Servicing Tools) (see Section 9701.4(a)); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Counsel could not process the foreclosure and/or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction in a manner that would successfully avoid the imposition of the transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: tax obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers will not be reimbursed for transfer taxes if any of the above conditions and
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements do not exist or are not met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the foreclosure involves a Manufactured Home in a certificate of title State, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must conduct the replevin or other legal action necessary to repossess the home
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Servicer’s name and have the new certificate of title issued in the Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Trust’s name.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA or RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must follow FHA and VA or RHS guidelines for conveying title to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosed property to the applicable agency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8601.7: Servicing high-risk, distressed and abandoned
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: properties (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The brown font text used throughout this chapter denotes the special requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages. Text in black or green font denotes general
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements that also apply elsewhere in the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing Mortgages on distressed properties and properties that pose a Risk of Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing Mortgages on abandoned properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicing Mortgages on distressed properties and properties that pose a Risk of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section has been adapted from Section 8403.1 to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are responsible for acting without delay and in an efficient and responsible manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: to protect the Servicer, Freddie Mac and the Senior Subordinate Trust’s interests when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer becomes aware of a Mortgaged Premises that becomes a distressed property. Refer
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Section 9401.1(b) for information regarding Freddie Mac’s rights when the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: becomes aware of a distressed property and fails to act to protect the Servicer’s, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s and the Senior Subordinate Trust’s interests as required by this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A distressed property is real property subject to a Mortgage that may or may not pose a Risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Property Ownership (see Section 9202.2(b)) to Freddie Mac or the Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust and is a property that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requires substantial repairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has sustained significant physical deterioration; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has been condemned by a local authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: Even if the owner has abandoned the Mortgaged Premises, a Servicer must report all Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgages on distressed properties that are 30 or more days delinquent via an
elements.label: text

elements.text: EDR transmission within the first three Business Days of the month following the month in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Servicer identified the problem using default reason code 011 (Property problem)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the applicable occupancy code. If the problem identified is contaminated drywall the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer should report default reason code 032 (Contaminated Drywall) rather than default
elements.label: text

elements.text: reason code 011 (Property problem).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a property is distressed, the Servicer must conduct a search of the records of the local code
elements.label: text

elements.text: authority to determine if there are any outstanding health or safety violations filed against
elements.label: text

elements.text: either the Borrower or the property. If a Servicer discovers any code violations on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: distressed property, the Servicer must report them to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) within
elements.label: text

elements.text: three Business Days of identifying the violation. The Servicer must attach a copy of the code
elements.label: text

elements.text: violation and copies of the most recent six consecutive months (or less, depending on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: level of Delinquency) of Form 1013, 1-4 Unit Property Inspection Report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to any reporting requirement set forth above for distressed properties, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Maintain a record of when the condition was discovered and take all necessary actions to
elements.label: text

elements.text: protect the property from waste, damage and vandalism and prevent any loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Inspect the property monthly until the condition is resolved
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Ensure that property insurance coverage is maintained. This includes taking all actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: required in the mortgage clause of all applicable property insurance policies, including,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not limited to, providing all notices to the insurer required under such clause, in order
elements.label: text

elements.text: to preserve the coverage and its maximum benefits for the Servicer and/or the Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Trust, as mortgagee
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. File a claim with the applicable property insurer on the Senior Subordinate Trust’s behalf
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the property is damaged and the Borrower has not filed a claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Comply with the requirements of the VA, RHS, FHA or MI, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must obtain Freddie Mac’s written pre-approval before incurring expenses on
elements.label: text

elements.text: distressed properties that exceed the limits contained in Exhibit 57, 1- to 4-Unit Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Approved Expense Amounts, by submitting a request for pre-approval via PAID (Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools). If unusual or
elements.label: text

elements.text: emergency circumstances do not allow a Servicer to request Freddie Mac’s prior written
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval, then the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via PAID by the next Business Day after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer incurred the expense. If the Servicer’s determination to incur the expense was
elements.label: text

elements.text: reasonable, as determined in Freddie Mac’s sole discretion, Freddie Mac will reimburse the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval to initiate foreclosure of a Senior
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate Mortgage on a distressed property as required in Section 9301.3(d). Approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: requests should be sent to Foreclosures@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a property securing a Senior Subordinate Mortgage has been identified as posing a Risk of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property Ownership, the Servicer must contact and report the Senior Subordinate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5), even if the Senior Subordinate Mortgage is not delinquent,
elements.label: text

elements.text: within three Business Days of identifying the risk. In such instances, the Servicer must attach
elements.label: text

elements.text: copies of the most recent six consecutive months (or less, depending on the level of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency) of Form 1013 and any other relevant information when reporting the Risk of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property Ownership to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicing Mortgages on abandoned properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must comply with the requirements of Section 8403.1(c), except that, with
elements.label: text

elements.text: respect to Senior Subordinate Mortgages, either the Servicer or the Senior Subordinate Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: would be the mortgagee and not Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8601.8: REO – asset management and compliance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The brown font text used throughout this chapter denotes the special requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Senior Subordinate Mortgages. Text in black or green font denotes general
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements that also apply elsewhere in the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ REO – General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ REO – Documentation and reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ REO – Notifying the taxing authority/homeowners association (HOA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) REO - General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9603.1(a) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Senior Subordinate Trust has acquired a property as an REO and after the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: has successfully reported the foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure in Resolve®
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to Section 9603.1(b), the Servicer will no longer be responsible for the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO activities, including for properties located in States that have a redemption,
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirmation process or ratification of sale:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Securing, maintaining, protecting, inspecting and preserving the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Making advances to superior lienholders, including condominium/HOA or Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation assessments (see Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans), Condominium Unit maintenance fees or Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maintenance Fees and ground rents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evicting the occupants in the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Marketing and rehabilitating the REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are responsible for the following activities pending the sale of the REO by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac or the MI, FHA, RHS or VA:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filing and concluding FHA, RHS and VA claims, if applicable. (Freddie Mac will file MI
elements.label: text

elements.text: claims.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Filing and concluding property insurance claims, if applicable, and applying for premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: refunds in accordance with Section 9603.1(d). The Servicer is required to cancel any
elements.label: text

elements.text: existing property insurance policies no later than 14 days after the foreclosure sale or
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure has been successfully reported to Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may instruct the Servicer to maintain property insurance (even if the property is vacant
elements.label: text

elements.text: and has no claimable damage). If Freddie Mac makes this request, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue the insurance coverage until Freddie Mac notifies the Servicer that Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: has sold the REO.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Referring all inquiries and offers regarding purchase of the REO to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 6) within one Business Day of the inquiry or offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fulfilling all requests made by Freddie Mac, including attorney selection. If the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: requires the assistance of an attorney in fulfilling any of the obligations set forth in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter, the Servicer must use an attorney who meets the criteria in Section 9501.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Taking such action that Freddie Mac may request regarding a property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may review the REO Overview report, accessible via the “REO” tile of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s Servicer Performance Profile (SPP) (see Exhibit 88), for the property status. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may also call Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE to obtain this information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not send the Servicer a written notice of the sale of the REO.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) REO – Documentation and reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9603.3(a) to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section details Freddie Mac’s requirements for submitting documentation and reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: information during the REO holding period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may review the REO Overview report, accessible via the “REO” tile of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s SPP (see Exhibit 88) for the property status, including the Property Condition
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate completion date. The Servicer may also call Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE
elements.label: text

elements.text: to obtain this information. Freddie Mac will not send the Servicer a written notice of the sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the REO.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) REO – Notifying the taxing authority/ HOA
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subsection has been adapted from Section 9603.1(c), to reflect special Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Senior Subordinate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within five days of successfully reporting the foreclosure sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Resolve, the Servicer must notify the taxing authority and condominium association, HOA
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Cooperative Corporation (see Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans), as applicable, of the Senior Subordinate Trust’s ownership of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property and Freddie Mac’s management of the property on behalf of the Senior Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust to ensure that statements are sent to the appropriate location.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer contacts these organizations, the Servicer must update the remitter’s name
elements.label: text

elements.text: as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: [Legal Name of Senior Subordinate Trust]
elements.label: text

elements.text: c/o Radian Real Estate Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7730 South Union Park Avenue, Suite 400
elements.label: text

elements.text: Midvale, UT 84047
elements.label: text

elements.text: Important: Freddie Mac will pay the property taxes, condominium/HOA or Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation assessments, Condominium Unit maintenance fees or Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maintenance Fees and ground rents, as applicable, as they become due after the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: successfully reports the foreclosure sale or the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: retain a vendor to conduct such activities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: There are special requirements for REO located in California. California taxing authorities
elements.label: text

elements.text: reassess properties for supplemental taxes from the foreclosure sale date to the sale date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the REO. Also, taxing authorities often generate a supplemental tax statement long after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO sale date. Therefore, the Servicer must notify the taxing authority in writing to send any
elements.label: text

elements.text: supplemental tax bills to the Senior Subordinate Trust in care of the address above within
elements.label: text

elements.text: five days following the foreclosure sale or the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8701: Servicing Requirements for
