elements.text: HeritageOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8901.1: Servicing HeritageOne® Mortgages (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Definitions used in this Chapter 8901
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer approval/eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tracking HeritageOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional Servicing requirements applicable to HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HeritageOne leasehold Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General notice and disclosure requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Occupancy attestation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subsequent Transfers of Servicing with respect to HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Definitions used in this Chapter 8901
elements.label: text

elements.text: Key terms and definitions used in this Chapter 8901 can be found below and in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4504.2(c). Servicers of a HeritageOne Mortgage should be familiar with these key terms and
elements.label: text

elements.text: definitions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Key terms and definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: BIA U.S. Department of the Interior Bureau of Indian Affairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: N
elements.label: text

elements.text: Native American Any person recognized as being Native American, American Indian
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Alaska Native by a Native American Tribe, the federal
elements.label: text

elements.text: government or any State
elements.label: text

elements.text: Native American
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tribe
elements.label: text

elements.text: A federally recognized Indian tribe of the United States that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the BIA’s most recent publication of the notice titled
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Indian Entities Recognized by and Eligible To Receive Services
elements.label: text

elements.text: From the United States Bureau of Indian Affairs”. “Native
elements.label: text

elements.text: American Tribe” includes any housing authority, utility,
elements.label: text

elements.text: corporation, partnership or other entity that is partially or wholly
elements.label: text

elements.text: owned by the Native American Tribe.
elements.label: text

elements.text: T
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tribal Area A census tract, or any portion thereof, that is designated as both a
elements.label: text

elements.text: HUD Indian Housing Block Grant (IHBG) Area and a rural area
elements.label: text

elements.text: under FHFA’s Duty to Serve Regulation and is identified as an
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Indian area” in the current version of FHFA’s Indian Areas File
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 4504 for specific requirements for selling a HeritageOne Mortgage to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicer approval/eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Chapter 8901 is not a general offering and is only available to specific Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by Freddie Mac due to the necessary expertise and additional requirements to
elements.label: text

elements.text: service HeritageOne Mortgages. A Seller/Servicer must either complete a Concurrent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Servicing or have explicit Freddie Mac approval as outlined below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer (Transferor Servicer) must complete a Concurrent Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing to a Transferee Servicer designated by Freddie Mac in accordance with Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7101 and the requirements of this Chapter 8901; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Seller/Servicer intends to retain the Servicing of HeritageOne Mortgages and fulfill
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing obligations in its capacity as Seller/Servicer, the Servicer must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: explicit approval from Freddie Mac to service HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Tracking HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers of HeritageOne Mortgages must implement policies, procedures and systems to
elements.label: text

elements.text: identify and track HeritageOne Mortgages to ensure that such Mortgages are serviced in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the Servicing requirements of the Guide and this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Additional Servicing requirements applicable to HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each HeritageOne Mortgage being serviced for Freddie Mac, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be approved to service loans on the Native American Tribe’s Tribal Area in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property resides, in accordance with applicable law; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Service HeritageOne Mortgages in accordance with the Servicing requirements of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide, this chapter and applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicing requirements of this chapter are applicable only to the Servicing of
elements.label: text

elements.text: HeritageOne Mortgages. If any Servicing requirement in this chapter is inconsistent with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirement set forth elsewhere in the Guide (except for requirements to comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with applicable law, which supersede any other Servicing requirement in the Guide), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must comply with the Servicing requirement set forth in this chapter with respect to
elements.label: text

elements.text: any HeritageOne Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must not apply any of the requirements set forth in this chapter to a Mortgage that
elements.label: text

elements.text: is not designated a HeritageOne Mortgage; rather, the Servicer should continue Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: such Mortgages in accordance with all other Servicing requirements of the Guide and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) HeritageOne leasehold Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless inconsistent with a Servicing requirement set forth in this Chapter 8901, all Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Guide governing Mortgages secured by leasehold estates (leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages) apply to HeritageOne leasehold Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional leasehold Mortgage requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements related to Servicing leasehold Mortgages if
elements.label: text

elements.text: termination of the lease will jeopardize Freddie Mac’s lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: position or interest in the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9301.6(d),
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for negotiating the extension of the current term of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: lease (or applicable renewal options) in the event the current term
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a lease (or applicable renewal options) terminates earlier than
elements.label: text

elements.text: five years after the maturity date of the proposed modification of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasehold Mortgage. As noted in Section 4504.8(b), this may
elements.label: text

elements.text: include obtaining approval from the U.S. Secretary of the Interior
elements.label: text

elements.text: or the BIA, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9206.1(c)(ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for HeritageOne leasehold Mortgages Section 4504.8
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: As described in Section 4504.5(h), HeritageOne Mortgages may be subject to resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restrictions. Unless inconsistent with a Servicing requirement set forth in this Chapter 8901,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should refer to Chapter 4406.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional resale restriction requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements regarding resale restrictions on HeritageOne
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4504.5(h)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale (“short sale”) evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale
elements.label: text

elements.text: restrictions, unless inconsistent with a Servicing requirement set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in this Chapter 8901
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9208.1(b)(iii) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure sale bidding requirements on properties subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: resale restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9301.8(a),
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) General notice and disclosure requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide all notices and disclosures to the Borrower obligated under a
elements.label: text

elements.text: HeritageOne Mortgage and, as applicable, the Native American Tribe, the BIA and/or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Secretary of the Interior as required by applicable law, the Mortgage documents and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable Freddie Mac HeritageOne memorandum of understanding, as outlined in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All notices and disclosures must include any required response requirements and/or time
elements.label: text

elements.text: frames.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Occupancy attestation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is highly encouraged, but not required, to have the Borrower obligated under a
elements.label: text

elements.text: HeritageOne Mortgage complete an annual attestation in which the Borrower attests to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current occupancy status of the property. Upon completion, the Servicer is encouraged to add
elements.label: text

elements.text: the attestation to the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Subsequent Transfers of Servicing with respect to HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: HeritageOne Mortgages must not be included in any Subsequent Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless the transfer is expressly approved by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8901.2: Delinquency management for HeritageOne®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower communication and single point of contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special Servicing and Early Delinquency Counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquency management process for HeritageOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Borrower communication and single point of contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the communication and outreach requirements for Delinquency as outlined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapters 9101 and 9102, Freddie Mac encourages, but does not require, the Servicer to use a
elements.label: text

elements.text: single point of contact model for communications with a Borrower obligated under a
elements.label: text

elements.text: HeritageOne Mortgage to ensure contact continuity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Special Servicing and Early Delinquency Counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise noted below, the requirements for special Servicing and Early Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: Counseling as described in Sections 9101.1 and 9102.4, as applicable, apply to HeritageOne
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, regardless of the Mortgage product type.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, Servicers may refer Borrowers obligated under a HeritageOne Mortgage who
elements.label: text

elements.text: are not located in a Duty to Serve high-needs area as listed in Exhibit 40, Duty to Serve
elements.label: text

elements.text: High-Needs Areas, for referral to Next Job re-employment services.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is not required to offer Early Delinquency Counseling if the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: knowledge or reason to believe that one of the following entities has established program
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements to offer its own Early Delinquency Counseling (e.g., analyzing the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial situation during a counseling session and developing a plan of action for solving the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency) and/or has already offered Early Delinquency Counseling to a delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower obligated under a HeritageOne Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Native American Tribe
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Native community development financial institution (CDFI), and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Tribal Designated Housing Entity associated with the applicable Native American
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tribe
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is not required to offer Early Delinquency Counseling, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: document the basis for not offering such counseling in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Delinquency management process for HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The steps below describe the process that Servicers must follow, in addition to the other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing requirements of the Guide, when a Borrower obligated under a HeritageOne
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is delinquent in his or her Mortgage payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Servicing requirements for HeritageOne Mortgages during Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Early Delinquency Counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pursuant to Section 8901.2(b), if applicable, Servicers of HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: must offer Early Delinquency Counseling as described in Sections 9101.1 and 9102.4,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Property inspections
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must order property inspections on the Mortgaged Premises in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 9202.3(c) and applicable law on all HeritageOne Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must meet any notification requirements per applicable law based upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: the location of the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with Section 9202.3(c), in the event that extenuating circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: cause property inspection delays, Servicers must document the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: explaining the cause of the delay(s) and any efforts to work with the Native American
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tribe to access the property and complete the property inspection.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Property preservation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must follow property preservation requirements for Abandoned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Properties as defined in and in accordance with Section 8403.1(c) and for properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: in foreclosure in accordance with Section 9301.6(d) for all HeritageOne Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must meet any notification requirements per applicable law based upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: the location of the Mortgaged Premises. In addition, if the property contains Native
elements.label: text

elements.text: American artifacts or remains, the Servicer must work with the Native American
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tribe or the BIA to ensure proper handling of the artifacts or remains when
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintaining the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D)The Native American Tribe’s rights during Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Native American Tribe may have certain rights if a Borrower subject to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: HeritageOne Mortgage becomes delinquent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with applicable law, Chapter 4504, the Mortgage documents and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable Freddie Mac HeritageOne memorandum of understanding, these rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: may include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Right of first refusal; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In the case of a HeritageOne leasehold Mortgage, curing the Delinquency on
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of the Borrower obligated under a HeritageOne Mortgage, or, if curing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency on behalf of the Borrower is determined by the lessor not to be a
elements.label: text

elements.text: viable solution to the Delinquency, paying off the HeritageOne leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage (e.g., by finding a new potential lessee to purchase the property from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower for an amount that pays off the total debt)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For additional requirements regarding right of first refusal on a HeritageOne
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, see Section 4504.5.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Preforeclosure referral Servicing requirements for HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Breach letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with Section 9101.2, Servicers must mail the breach letter for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: HeritageOne Mortgages, including First Lien Mortgages and modified Mortgages, no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than the 75th day of Delinquency (i.e., 105 days after the DDLPI).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If applicable law requires the breach letter (or any other pre-breach letter or notice) to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be sent prior to the 75th day of Delinquency in order to be able to refer the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: to foreclosure as required in Sections 9301.2(c) and 9301.2(d), the Servicer must send
elements.label: text

elements.text: the breach letter on such earlier date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must provide a copy of the breach letter to all parties as required by
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law, which may include, but are not limited to, the Borrower obligated
elements.label: text

elements.text: under a HeritageOne Mortgage, Native American Tribe, any tribal entity named in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: right of first refusal, BIA and/or the Secretary of the Interior.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Preforeclosure referral account review
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 15 days prior to foreclosure referral, the Servicer must review the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file to ensure that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has made every attempt to achieve quality right party contact in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 9102.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least one Borrower Solicitation Package or Borrower Solicitation Letter has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been sent by the 45th day after the Due Date of an unpaid monthly installment
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the response period has expired without an affirmative Borrower response
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is neither an approved payment arrangement nor an alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure offer pending for which the Borrower response period has not expired
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The breach letter (1) has been issued and expired and (2) has been provided to all
elements.label: text

elements.text: required parties in accordance with applicable law, which may include, but are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, the Borrower obligated under a HeritageOne Mortgage, Native
elements.label: text

elements.text: American Tribe, any tribal entity named in a right of first refusal, the BIA and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Secretary of the Interior
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If applicable, any additional required notices or demand letters, with the required
elements.label: text

elements.text: time frame for response included, have been provided to appropriate parties in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with applicable law, any applicable Freddie Mac HeritageOne
elements.label: text

elements.text: memorandum of understanding and the Mortgage documents. Such appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties may include, but are not limited to, the Borrower obligated under a
elements.label: text

elements.text: HeritageOne Mortgage, Native American Tribe, any tribal entity named in a right
elements.label: text

elements.text: of first refusal, the BIA and/or the Secretary of the Interior. Additionally, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must confirm the response time frame provided in the notices has
elements.label: text

elements.text: expired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Foreclosure Servicing requirements for HeritageOne Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Additional notices post referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pursuant to applicable law, any applicable Freddie Mac HeritageOne memorandum of
elements.label: text

elements.text: understanding and the Mortgage documents, the Servicer must provide any required
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure-related notices to the Native American Tribe, and any other required
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties, that describe how the Servicer may express its intention to exercise any
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforcement rights available to it under applicable law or the Mortgage documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon delivery of the foreclosure-related notices to the required parties, the required
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties must give written notice to the Servicer of their intention to exercise any rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: available to them under applicable law and the Mortgage documents within the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: frame specified by the Servicer. Unless otherwise prohibited per applicable law, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: such timely written notice is given to the Servicer, the Servicer may, upon receipt and
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with applicable law and the Mortgage documents, suspend foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceedings to allow the required party(ies) to exercise its rights. If a foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: date is scheduled, the Servicer may, to the extent possible and in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law, extend the foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) HeritageOne Mortgage foreclosure – State jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the State holds jurisdiction in the Mortgage proceedings (e.g., if the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is owned in fee simple and located within exterior boundaries of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reservation area) or the applicable tribal court chooses to apply State Mortgage laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: and procedures to its foreclosure proceedings, then the Servicer must follow the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Chapter 9301 unless inconsistent with the Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)HeritageOne Mortgage Foreclosure – tribal jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the foreclosure must be conducted through tribal court, the Servicer must follow the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Chapters 9301 and 8901 and all applicable tribal law and tribal court
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D)Tribal jurisdiction foreclosure Counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the foreclosure must be processed through tribal court, the Servicer must refer
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure to Counsel that meets the Firm Minimum Requirements, as specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 9501.2, and is approved to appear in the Native American Tribe’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: reservation and applicable tribal courts in accordance with applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event that extenuating circumstances cause excessive delays in foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing on a HeritageOne Mortgage, Servicers are instructed to document the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file explaining the cause of the delays.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9101: Delinquency Management for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages Secured by Primary Residences
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9101.1: Servicer collection efforts for Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: secured by Primary Residences (12/01/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter sets forth Servicing requirements relating to collection efforts, Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: communications, foreclosure postponement and management of an appeals process in connection
elements.label: text

elements.text: with a denial of a Trial Period Plan for Mortgages secured by Primary Residences. Except as
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise noted in this chapter, Servicers must refer to Chapters 9102 through 9401 and Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer collection efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers on automatic withdrawal (Automated Clearing House (ACH))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum collection efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use of a Collection and Loss Mitigation Tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special Servicing and Early Delinquency Counseling for Home Possible® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicer collection efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must treat each Delinquency individually by varying the collection techniques
elements.label: text

elements.text: to fit the individual circumstances. The Servicer must avoid establishing a fixed routine; this
elements.label: text

elements.text: is particularly ineffective when dealing with chronically delinquent Borrowers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All collection efforts must be based on the extent of the Delinquency and the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: knowledge of the following factors:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s prior payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s credit history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s employment situation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s reason for default
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s willingness and ability to repay
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower is deceased or the property is included as part of the Borrower’s estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgaged Premises’ condition
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgaged Premises’ occupancy status
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there is a known potential risk of loss or ownership to Freddie Mac, the Borrower must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: referred for loss mitigation immediately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Borrowers on automatic withdrawal (ACH)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should encourage Borrowers to set up ACH payment arrangements. If a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreed to have his or her monthly payments automatically withdrawn from a bank account,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must ensure that the correct monthly payment is withdrawn (especially after an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow analysis has taken place) and processed timely. If the ACH payment cannot be
elements.label: text

elements.text: processed on the specified date the Borrower has agreed to, the Servicer must begin efforts to
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact the Borrower within 48 hours to determine the reason for the missed payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Minimum collection efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must, at a minimum:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be readily available to the Borrower to offer financial counseling and advice on curing
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Delinquency and explaining alternatives to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Make personal contact with the Borrower as early and often as necessary to promptly
elements.label: text

elements.text: cure the Delinquency. Mortgages in bankruptcy or litigation may be excluded if
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary under applicable law, rules of professional responsibility or court rules.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Servicers are authorized to use alternative outreach methods to contact the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower as permitted by applicable law including, but not limited to, e-mail, text
elements.label: text

elements.text: messaging or voice response unit technology.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Continue to contact the Borrower if satisfactory arrangements have not been made to cure
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Delinquency or until the Servicer determines foreclosure is appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Issue the breach letter (may also be referred to as the “notice of acceleration” or “demand
elements.label: text

elements.text: letter”) for all Mortgages no later than the 75th day of Delinquency. If State law requires
elements.label: text

elements.text: the breach letter (or any other pre-breach letter or notice) to be sent prior to the 75th day
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Delinquency in order to be able to refer the Mortgage to foreclosure as required in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9301.2(c) and 9301.2(d) the Servicer must send the breach letter on such earlier
elements.label: text

elements.text: date.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 9202.3(c)(iv), then the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Issue a breach letter within 10 days of the determination, provided the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater than 30 days delinquent; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Refer all Mortgages secured by Primary Residences to foreclosure after expiration of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the breach letter, but no earlier than 151 days from the DDLPI (121st day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency). Refer to Sections 9301.2(c) and 9301.2(d) for additional requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to foreclosure referral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contact the lessor for leasehold Mortgages and any lender with a prior lien on the fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: simple landowner/lessor’s fee simple interest

elements.text: (d) Use of a Collection and Loss Mitigation Tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may use their own methodology or a tool that uses statistical models to predict
elements.label: text

elements.text: worsening Delinquency and use the results of the tool to tailor its collection efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“Collection and Loss Mitigation Tool”), to determine when contact attempts should begin.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, regardless of the methodology or tool employed, Servicers must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum collection time frames in this section even if a Collection and Loss Mitigation Tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: is used, including initiating contact attempts no later than 36 days after the Due Date of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: unpaid monthly installment. Servicers using a Collection and Loss Mitigation Tool when
elements.label: text

elements.text: managing contact attempts must make model specifications and code available to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must conduct periodic reviews to ensure the effectiveness of the Collection and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loss Mitigation Tool, including compliance with applicable laws, such as anti-discriminatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: laws. Freddie Mac reserves the right to require a Servicer to discontinue the use of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Collection and Loss Mitigation Tool for Freddie Mac Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Special Servicing and Early Delinquency Counseling for Home Possible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Home Possible Mortgages (see Chapter 4501), Servicers are required to offer tailored
elements.label: text

elements.text: support through special Servicing and Early Delinquency Counseling, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special Servicing — Welcome Letter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must send the Borrower a letter, shortly after closing and before the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage payment is due, stressing the importance of making timely payments and
elements.label: text

elements.text: advising the Borrower to contact the Servicer if he or she experiences any financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: problems. The letter may be incorporated into the welcome letter sent to Borrowers after
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing. The letter must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Instructions on how to contact the Servicer if the Borrower is having difficulty
elements.label: text

elements.text: making the Mortgage payment on time
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer’s business hours
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A toll-free telephone number to reach the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Early Delinquency Counseling.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following provisions apply to Early Delinquency Counseling:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Early Delinquency Counseling is counseling provided to a delinquent Borrower by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: nonprofit third-party homeownership-counseling agency or an eligible Servicer that
elements.label: text

elements.text: involves identifying the reason(s) a Borrower did not make a Mortgage payment on
elements.label: text

elements.text: time and working with the Borrower to resolve any financial problems so that future
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage payments can be made on a timely basis. The counseling includes a
elements.label: text

elements.text: personal and interactive relationship with the Borrower that deals with money
elements.label: text

elements.text: management, budgeting and debt management counseling.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Servicers must offer Early Delinquency Counseling including household budget
elements.label: text

elements.text: management counseling at no charge to delinquent Borrowers for each Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: that occurs during the first year following the Note Date. In addition, if at any time
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the life of the Mortgage, the Borrower is having difficulty making Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments, the Servicer should advise the Borrower to speak with the Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact a counseling agency for further assistance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer must provide counseling in accordance with at least one of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: options:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A nonprofit third-party homeownership counseling agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Servicer. The Servicer may conduct the counseling provided the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies and procedures in place to offer the same kind of comprehensive
elements.label: text

elements.text: counseling, budgeting and advising capabilities as a counseling agency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A HUD-approved national counseling agency specified by Freddie Mac. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may use this option to refer delinquent Borrowers with Home Possible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages to Freddie Mac for counseling services provided by a national
elements.label: text

elements.text: counseling agency without charge to the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To refer a delinquent Borrower for counseling services provided by an agency, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must complete the “Counseling Agency-Servicer Referral” template in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the instructions provided in the template and send the referral via
elements.label: text

elements.text: secure e-mail to Freddie Mac at FM_Counseling_Outreach@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The template is accessible at https://sf.freddiemac.com/docs/xls/fact￾sheet/counseling_servicer_referral.xls. The template contains instructions for
elements.label: text

elements.text: carrying out referrals to the agencies and for identifying Mortgages that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: ineligible for referral. After Freddie Mac receives the referral, Freddie Mac and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the agency will contact the Borrower to offer counseling services to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For more information about the network of national counseling agencies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should visit https://myhome.freddiemac.com/resources/housing-help￾centers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer relies on a counseling agency, it must be fully aware of the status or
elements.label: text

elements.text: outcome of all counseling efforts the counseling agency undertakes with a specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Initial contact with delinquent Borrower. If the Borrower is experiencing a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Send a letter advising the Borrower of the availability of free counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contact the delinquent Borrower to determine the Borrower’s current financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: situation and the reason for the Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Counseling Process and Actions. The counseling must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Analyzing the Borrower’s financial situation and developing a plan of action for
elements.label: text

elements.text: solving the Delinquency, which in most cases will be a budget worksheet or
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout plan giving priority to the Mortgage payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Developing a budget and debt repayment plan enabling the Borrower to meet his
elements.label: text

elements.text: or her financial obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reviewing the budget worksheet or workout plan with the Borrower and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer, if applicable, so a decision can be made on how to proceed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ In addition to minimum collection efforts set forth in this section, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer Early Delinquency Counseling no later than the 30th day after the Due Date and
elements.label: text

elements.text: schedule or conduct the initial counseling session with the Borrower no later than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: 45th day after the Due Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Servicer or a third-party non-profit homeownership counseling agency chosen
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Servicer provides the counseling, the Servicer must include in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the “Welcome Letter” as described in Section 9102.4(c)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The date(s) that counseling was offered
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s response(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The name of the counseling agency providing the counseling (if not the Servicer)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A brief summary of the results of the counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Servicer utilizes a HUD-approved national counseling agency specified by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac to provide the counseling, as outlined above, the Servicer must include
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the “Welcome Letter” as described in Section 9102.4(c)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The results of the counseling outreach as provided by Freddie Mac or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified HUD national counseling agency, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Transfer of Servicing. If the Mortgage is included in a Transfer of Servicing before
elements.label: text

elements.text: the end of the one-year period during which Early Delinquency Counseling is
elements.label: text

elements.text: required, the transferee Servicer must be informed of the requirement and must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: able to provide the required counseling or make arrangements for a counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency, as necessary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9101.2: Evaluation requirements and appeals process
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: in response to the First Complete Borrower Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section describes the requirements a Servicer must undertake to facilitate an appeals process
elements.label: text

elements.text: when the Borrower is denied a Trial Period Plan based on the Servicer’s evaluation of the First
elements.label: text

elements.text: Complete Borrower Response Package. The appeals process described in this section does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: apply to evaluation decisions that are offered based on a review of an incomplete or no Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluation timing and communication to Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Managing the Borrower’s response to an evaluation decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower acceptance of an original offer after an appeal decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Trial Period payment due date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document retention
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Evaluation timing and communication to Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within five days of an evaluation decision, but no later than 30 days following receipt of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: First Complete Borrower Response Package, the Servicer must provide the appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Evaluation Notice as set forth in Section 9101.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer receives a complete Borrower Response Package 90 or more days before a
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled foreclosure sale date or prior to the sale date being set, the Servicer must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appropriate Borrower Evaluation Notice, which informs the Borrower of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The option to either accept or reject an offer of an alternative to foreclosure and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional option to appeal within 14 days of the date of the evaluation notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The right to accept an offer of an alternative to foreclosure and simultaneously appeal a
elements.label: text

elements.text: denial of a Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The right to accept any offer of an alternative to foreclosure for which the Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved as a result of the appeal or to accept or indicate an intent to accept any original
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer of an alternative to foreclosure, which will still be available as an option up to 14
elements.label: text

elements.text: days after the appeal decision has been sent to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any determination of ineligibility for the Freddie Mac Flex Modification® upon review
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a complete Borrower Response Package; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The right to appeal a denial of a Trial Period Plan and the process and timeline,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The address to which the Borrower must submit the appeal, which must be in writing
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must include the Borrower’s name, property address and Mortgage loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A statement that the Servicer’s appeal determination is not subject to further appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A statement that the foreclosure process will be suspended during the appeals process
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must send the Borrower the appropriate Borrower Evaluation Notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: communicating the decision with one of the following outcomes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Offer reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Offer a repayment plan (with or without a partial reinstatement offer)*
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Offer a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Offer a Trial Period Plan as set forth in Chapter 9206 (e.g., Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Offer a short sale as set forth in Chapter 9208 (i.e., Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Offer a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure as set forth in Chapter 9209 (i.e., Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-Approval, Ineligible for Mortgage Assistance – Mortgage is Current
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-Approval, Ineligible for Mortgage Assistance – Mortgage is Delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Model letters describing each of these responses are included in Exhibit 93, Evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notices. Use of the model letters is optional; however, they illustrate the level of specificity
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is deemed to be in compliance with the requirements of this section. The model letters
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be altered at the Servicer’s discretion to meet the requirements of this chapter and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapters 8404, 9203, 9206, 9208 and 9209 and must be altered by the Servicer as necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: to comply with applicable federal, State and local law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must ensure that the model letters are revised as necessary to include denial reasons
elements.label: text

elements.text: and information about the Borrower’s right to appeal the Servicer’s decision when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is denied a Trial Period Plan based on an evaluation of the First Complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package. Exhibit 93 includes a list of denial reasons for use with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: model letters.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As a reminder, impermissible denial reasons include, but are not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Full lender recourse or indemnification – In situations where the Servicer chose not to
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase the Mortgage from Freddie Mac or, in the case of indemnification, chose not
elements.label: text

elements.text: to modify the Mortgage, the Servicer must disclose the reason it will not modify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, which must be based on its own requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are reminded that they may repurchase any Mortgage subject to full lender
elements.label: text

elements.text: recourse or indemnification in accordance with the requirements set forth in Bulletin
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2012-10 and may choose to modify a Mortgage subject to indemnification in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the requirements in Bulletins 2016-5 and 2021-14.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA, RHS or VA Mortgage – The Servicer must determine eligibility for a loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification under applicable agency requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may combine the relevant contents of the Borrower Evaluation Notice for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstatement offer with the contents of the notice for a repayment plan offer when offering
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower a partial reinstatement of the Mortgage coupled with a repayment plan in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of Chapter 9203.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Furthermore, if the offer for a foreclosure alternative is made 37 days or less prior to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled foreclosure sale as set forth in Section 9301.7(a), a Borrower’s intent to accept the
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer may not be communicated verbally but must be in writing. Therefore, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: delete the reference “at [SERVICER PHONE NUMBER] or” and otherwise amend the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evaluation Notice as appropriate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with applicable law when obtaining a complete Borrower Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Managing the Borrower’s response to an evaluation decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may only accept and review a valid appeal of the denial of any Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plan based on the evaluation of the First Complete Borrower Response Package within 14
elements.label: text

elements.text: days from the date of the Servicer’s offer or denial notice. A valid appeal requires the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower to provide the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An appeal of the denial for a Trial Period Plan in writing with his or her name
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property address; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any Borrower appeal must be reviewed by different Servicer personnel than those
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for the initial evaluation decision. The Borrower may, but is not required to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit any supporting documentation at the time he or she notifies the Servicer of an appeal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must adhere to the following guidelines regarding the submission and evaluation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: supporting documentation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Borrower submits supporting documentation within 14 days of the date the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: sent the Borrower Evaluation Notice, the Servicer must evaluate the supporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation during the appeal review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If documentation presented causes the Borrower to no longer be eligible for an original
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure, then the Servicer must inform the Borrower that the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: is no longer eligible for that original alternative to foreclosure. The model clauses in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 93 include language that conditions the offers on continuing eligibility based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: information known to the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Borrower submits new information or documentation after the 14-day Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: response period, the new information or documentation is not subject to the right of
elements.label: text

elements.text: appeal process in this section and must be evaluated in accordance with Section 9201.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, the Servicer may consider such new information as part of the appeal process
elements.label: text

elements.text: within the time frames described in this section if it is feasible to do so.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must have a policy applied consistently to all Mortgages to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether any new information received after the appeal decision is a material change in
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstance that warrants another review. Servicers must treat any new information and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation received after the 14-day Borrower response period as a new Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer concludes that a review of the updated complete Borrower Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package is warranted, the Servicer must provide a decision to the Borrower within 30
elements.label: text

elements.text: days of receipt of the new information as provided under Section 9102.5(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must provide the Borrower with an appeal decision notice within 30 days of
elements.label: text

elements.text: receipt of the Borrower’s appeal. The appeal decision must include the original offer, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: any, provided the Borrower remains eligible for the original offer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Borrower is eligible for a Trial Period Plan with different terms or that was not part
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the original offer, the Servicer must provide the new Trial Period Plan to that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower. The Servicer must provide the Borrower with 14 days (from the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer sends the appeal decision notice) to accept either the original offer, if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower remains eligible, or indicate an intent to accept the new Trial Period Plan offer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a payment is required under an offer, then the Servicer must follow the relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements under Chapters 9203, 9205, 9206, 9208 and 9209 concerning legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptance for that offer. However, the Servicer must continue to suspend foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: until the deadline for submission of the first payment under that offer has passed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Borrower acceptance of an original offer after an appeal decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower accepts the original offer after receiving the appeal decision and remains
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for the original offer, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reissue the original offer extended in the initial Borrower Evaluation Notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reissue the original offer with a new first Trial Period Plan due date, if applicable, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Inform the Borrower that unpaid interest and other amounts that continue to accrue
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the appeal, such as Escrows for taxes and insurance, will result in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage becoming more delinquent; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Inform the Borrower that if the Trial Period Plan is successfully completed, then, at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time of modification, any additional arrearages will be capitalized into the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modified UPB, if permitted by applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the original offer is reissued to the Borrower, the Servicer may either revise the Trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period payment amount or keep it the same provided it applies the same method consistently
elements.label: text

elements.text: to all similarly situated Borrowers for the Freddie Mac Mortgages it services. However, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must ensure the final terms of the modification reflect all arrearages, including any
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional delinquent amounts accrued during the appeal review process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Trial Period payment due date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a new Trial Period Plan offer is made with the appeal decision, the Trial Period payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: due date must be set in accordance with Section 9206.2(b), assuming that there is a new Trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period Plan offer being made. The period for the Borrower to indicate an intent to accept the
elements.label: text

elements.text: new Trial Period Plan offer is extended until 14 days following the new Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer, and legal acceptance via payment, as required under the Trial Period Plan, remains
elements.label: text

elements.text: unchanged.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Trial Period payment due date of a new Trial Period Plan offer or revised payment due
elements.label: text

elements.text: date for an original Trial Period Plan offer is based on when the appeals decision is sent by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer to the Borrower. The Servicer must determine the Trial Period Plan Effective
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date and the due date of the first Trial Period payment in accordance with the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: instructions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer sends the appeal decision on or before the 15th of the month, then the Trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period Plan Effective Date and the due date of the first Trial Period payment is the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: day of the next month
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer sends the appeal decision after the 15th of the month, then the Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plan Effective Date and the Due Date of the first Trial Period payment is the first day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the month after the next month
elements.label: text

elements.text: For example, if the Servicer sends the appeal decision notice to the Borrower between June 1
elements.label: text

elements.text: and June 15, the Trial Period Plan Effective Date and first Trial Period payment due date are
elements.label: text

elements.text: both July 1. If the Servicer sends the appeal decision notice to the Borrower between June 16
elements.label: text

elements.text: and June 30, the Trial Period Plan Effective Date and the first Trial Period payment due date
elements.label: text

elements.text: are both August 1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In response to the Servicer’s decision, the Borrower’s notification to the Servicer that he or
elements.label: text

elements.text: she intends to accept an offer may be demonstrated as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verbal notification, as permitted in Sections 9101.3 and 9301.7(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notification; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remittance of payment due under the offer of an alternative to foreclosure that requires
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment (e.g., a Trial Period Plan)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See also foreclosure postponement requirements in Section 9101.3 in response to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation decision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Document retention
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain in the Mortgage file documentation of all communications
elements.label: text

elements.text: relating to appeals. The Servicer must provide appeal case files or aggregated information to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for review upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General foreclosure suspension obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Acceptance of offer requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale (“short sale”) evaluations and foreclosure suspension
elements.label: text

elements.label: text
