elements.label: text

elements.text: suspension obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General foreclosure suspension obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with the following foreclosure suspension requirements for the First
elements.label: text

elements.text: Complete Borrower Response Package received more than 37 days prior to a scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale. However, existing foreclosure suspension requirements set forth in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.7(a) still apply when:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The First Complete Borrower Response Package is received 37 days or less prior to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is not secured by a Primary Residence; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is secured by a Primary Residence and the Servicer is reviewing a
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete Borrower Response Package received after the First Complete Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must delay or ensure that foreclosure counsel delays filing the first legal action
elements.label: text

elements.text: (as defined in Section 9301.5(b)), the motion for judgment or order of sale or (if the motion
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been filed) request the court to delay a hearing or ruling as permitted under State or local
elements.label: text

elements.text: law, unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The First Complete Borrower Response Package was received 90 or more days prior to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled foreclosure sale or received at any time prior to a foreclosure sale date being
elements.label: text

elements.text: set, and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer has reviewed the First Complete Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer notified the Borrower of its decision, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Borrower has not requested an appeal within 14 days of the Servicer’s evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision or the Borrower’s appeal has been denied
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has reviewed the First Complete Borrower Response Package received less
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 90 days prior to the foreclosure sale date and notified the Borrower of a denial
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision. In this instance, the Servicer’s decision is not subject to an appeal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower rejects an alternative to foreclosure offered by the Servicer; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower fails to perform under the terms of an alternative to foreclosure, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: missing a Trial Period Plan payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Acceptance of offer requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower’s notification to the Servicer that he or she intends to accept an offer may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: demonstrated as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verbal notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, if a payment is required to accept an alternative to foreclosure offer and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower does not communicate a verbal or written rejection of the offer, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: postpone foreclosure actions where legally permitted until after the last day of the month in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the first payment is due under the terms of the alternative to foreclosure offer. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer receives the first payment timely in accordance with the terms of a Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plan, repayment plan or forbearance plan, the Servicer must delay the next legal action unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower breaches the plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale (“short sale”) evaluations and foreclosure suspension
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements describe the actions a Servicer must take when the First
elements.label: text

elements.text: Complete Borrower Response Package is received more than 37 days prior to a scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale and results in an offer to proceed with a short sale:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must provide a decision to the Borrower within five days of an evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision but no later than 30 days from the Servicer’s receipt of the complete Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must include in the evaluation notice a date by which the Borrower must
elements.label: text

elements.text: respond to the short sale offer (including a counteroffer to the purchase offer received),
elements.label: text

elements.text: which must be 14 days from the date of the Borrower Evaluation Notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a purchase offer was not submitted with the First Complete Borrower Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer must require the Borrower to provide a purchase offer no later than 45
elements.label: text

elements.text: days from the date of the Borrower Evaluation Notice or the date of an appeal
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision, if applicable, for purposes of delaying the next legal action in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer must communicate to the Borrower that if a purchase contract is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: received by the due date, which is 45 days from the date of the Borrower Evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notice, the Borrower can continue to market the property after the expiration of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: due date; however, foreclosure legal actions will commence or recommence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Once a purchase offer is received within 45 days from the date of a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evaluation Notice, the Servicer must complete its review and provide a decision to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower within 15 days of receipt of the purchase offer and, if the Servicer sends
elements.label: text

elements.text: a counteroffer, it must require the Borrower to respond to the counteroffer within five
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days of the date the Borrower receives the counteroffer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must delay or ensure that foreclosure counsel delays filing the first legal action,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the motion for judgment or order of sale or (if the motion has been filed) request the court to
elements.label: text

elements.text: delay a hearing or ruling as permitted under State or local law during the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The evaluation of the First Complete Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower response period to the Servicer’s evaluation decision (i.e., 14 days from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the date of the Borrower Evaluation Notice)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The 45-day response period in which the Borrower must provide a purchase offer to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer, provided the Borrower responded timely to the Servicer’s evaluation decision
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer review period (up to 15 days) to provide a decision on a purchase offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: received within 45 days from the date of a Borrower Evaluation Notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appeal period, if applicable, if the Borrower exercises his or her right to appeal a
elements.label: text

elements.text: denial of a loan modification. The Servicer must delay the next legal action during its
elements.label: text

elements.text: review of the Borrower’s appeal and up to an additional 14 days for the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: respond to the appeal decision. In cases where a purchase offer was not submitted with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the First Complete Borrower Response Package, the Servicer must adjust the time frame
elements.label: text

elements.text: in which the purchase offer must be submitted, which is 45 days from the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appeal decision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer approves a purchase offer received within 45 days of the date of the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evaluation Notice, it must continue to delay filing the first legal action, the motion for
elements.label: text

elements.text: judgment or order of sale or foreclosure sale to permit settlement of the short sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the purchase offer is rejected and the Borrower does not assert his or her right to appeal for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a loan modification or the purchase offer is not received within 45 days from the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Evaluation Notice, the Servicer must proceed with the next legal action in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure process. However, the Borrower may continue to market the property while
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure legal actions commence or recommence. If a purchase offer is eventually
elements.label: text

elements.text: received, the Servicer must provide a decision within 30 days of receipt.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 9208.1(b) through 9208.3 for additional requirements for completing
elements.label: text

elements.text: a short sale and requirements specific to situations where the Servicer is not evaluating the
elements.label: text

elements.text: First Complete Borrower Response Package received more than 37 days prior to a scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Freddie Mac Standard Deed-in-Lieu of foreclosure (“DIL”) evaluations and foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspension obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements describe the actions a Servicer must take when the First
elements.label: text

elements.text: Complete Borrower Response Package is received more than 37 days prior to a scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale and results in an offer to proceed with a DIL:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must provide a decision to the Borrower within five days of an evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision but no later than 30 days from the Servicer’s receipt of the complete Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must include in the evaluation notice a date by which the Borrower must
elements.label: text

elements.text: respond to the DIL offer, which must be 14 days from the date of the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evaluation Notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Borrower is approved to proceed with the DIL and accepts the offer, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must receive all necessary approvals (e.g., title, secondary lien(s), MI) and execute the
elements.label: text

elements.text: DIL within 60 days of the date the Borrower’s communication to accept the offer was
elements.label: text

elements.text: received by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that foreclosure counsel delays filing the first legal action, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: motion for judgment or order of sale or (if the motion has been filed) request the court to
elements.label: text

elements.text: delay a hearing or ruling as permitted under State or local law during:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The evaluation of the complete Borrower Response Package in which the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide a decision within five days of an evaluation decision but no later than 30 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: from receipt of the Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower response period to the Servicer’s evaluation decision (14 days from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of the Borrower Evaluation Notice)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appeal period, if applicable, if the Borrower exercises his or her right to appeal for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan modification. The Servicer must delay the next legal action during its review of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s appeal (up to 30 days) and up to an additional 14 days for the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: respond to the appeal decision. The Servicer must adjust the time frame in which to delay
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure actions for up to 60 days to complete the DIL, to be based on the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower acceptance of an offer after an appeal decision has been communicated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completion of the DIL transaction within 60 days of the date the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: communicated acceptance of the DIL offer. The Servicer must continue to delay the next
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal action in the foreclosure process to complete the DIL provided that the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: did not previously accept a short sale offer that was not viable and then subsequently
elements.label: text

elements.text: accepts the DIL offer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is unable to complete the DIL transaction within 60 days of the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower acceptance, the Servicer must proceed with the next legal action in the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: process. The Servicer will be allocated an additional 30 days to complete the DIL transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must only delay the next legal action in the foreclosure process upon receipt of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed deed and all required DIL documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, if the Borrower was previously approved for a short sale and transitioned to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: DIL because the short sale transaction was not successful, the Servicer must not delay the
elements.label: text

elements.text: next legal action in the foreclosure process until the Servicer has received the executed deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and all required DIL documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 9209.1 through 9209.6 for additional requirements related to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion of a DIL, and requirements specific to situations where the Servicer is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluating the First Complete Borrower Response Package received more than 37 days prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a scheduled foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9102: Delinquencies
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9102.1: General requirements for Servicing delinquent
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquency rates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Collection records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Funds in buydown accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA, RHS, VA and MI requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remitting compensatory and other fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Receiving reimbursement of expenses/payment of incentives via Automated Clearing House
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ACH) credit entries
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicer responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must develop, follow and maintain prudent and efficient written procedures that
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet Freddie Mac requirements for promptly curing Delinquencies and comply with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable laws. In addition:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must employ staff that is experienced and skilled in financial counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Mortgage collection techniques. The Servicer’s staff must assist a Borrower in
elements.label: text

elements.text: bringing a Mortgage current while protecting the Borrower’s equity and credit rating and
elements.label: text

elements.text: protecting Freddie Mac’s interests. The Servicer must also employ staff that is adequately
elements.label: text

elements.text: trained to discuss interest rate adjustments for Step-Rate Mortgages, in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements in Chapter 8501.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac encourages Servicers to develop a Borrower Delinquency management
elements.label: text

elements.text: model that allows a Borrower to contact one individual or a dedicated team of individuals
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Servicer’s organization to obtain accurate information on the various alternatives to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure available to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer develops a Borrower Delinquency management model, the individual or
elements.label: text

elements.text: dedicated team of individuals should also be able to handle and resolve Borrower issues
elements.label: text

elements.text: throughout the Delinquency management process and provide updates on the status of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any request for an alternative to foreclosure and the status of pending foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Delinquency rates
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s continued eligibility to service Mortgages for Freddie Mac is contingent on
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintaining Delinquency rates that are acceptable to Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: disqualify or suspend a Servicer if the Servicer’s 30-, 60- or 90+-day Delinquency rate or
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO rate exceeds the national or regional average (e.g., Standard Metropolitan Statistical
elements.label: text

elements.text: Area, county or State in which the Mortgaged Premises is located) by more than 50% for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages owned or guaranteed by Freddie Mac with similar Mortgage and Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: characteristics (e.g., origination year, loan-to-value ratio and documentation type (full
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation, reduced documentation).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Collection records
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain records of all collection efforts and make the records available
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Freddie Mac’s inspection on request. The Servicer’s collection records must evidence:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Dates that letters and notices were sent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Dates and results of personal contacts with the Borrower via telephone, e-mail, face-to￾face or other responsible collection techniques
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reasons for prior and current defaults
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Terms of any repayment arrangements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation of property inspections, as required in Section 9202.3(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Funds in buydown accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not apply funds in a buydown account to bring a delinquent Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: current unless it is otherwise required per the Guide or the terms of the applicable buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) FHA, RHS, VA and MI requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must be familiar with and satisfy all applicable requirements of the FHA, RHS,
elements.label: text

elements.text: VA or MI. The Servicer is required to file on a timely basis all notices to the FHA, RHS, VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: or MI. Copies of routine notices sent to the FHA, RHS, VA, MI or Borrower must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintained in the Mortgage file but need not be sent to Freddie Mac unless specifically
elements.label: text

elements.text: requested.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each notice to the MI must state that Freddie Mac is the owner of each related Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Freddie Mac rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac retains the right in its discretion to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Impose additional Servicing requirements as Freddie Mac deems appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Direct the Servicer to transfer Servicing of any delinquent Mortgage serviced for Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac that has two or more payments outstanding as of a date in the month specified by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (the “Determination Date”) to a Servicer designated by Freddie Mac. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Servicing of delinquent Mortgages will occur monthly, effective as of a date
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the month specified by Freddie Mac (the “Transfer Date”). Any Mortgage identified as
elements.label: text

elements.text: two or more payments in arrears on the Determination Date will transfer on the Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date, regardless of whether the loan becomes current before the Transfer Date. Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will provide the Servicer (the Transferor Servicer) with written notice at least 90
elements.label: text

elements.text: days in advance of the first Transfer of Servicing date for such Mortgages. With respect
elements.label: text

elements.text: to such a Transfer of Servicing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicing compensation for the Mortgages being transferred shall be determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac and shall be payable to the new Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ No compensation will be paid to the Transferor Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Transferor Servicer will be responsible for any transfer-related expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 3603.1(a)(i)(A) for a description of such expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Such Mortgages will remain with the Transferee Servicer, whether or not they
elements.label: text

elements.text: become current after the Determination Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transfer of Servicing does not relieve the Transferor Servicer of any of its
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations under the Purchase Documents with respect to the Mortgages for which
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing is transferred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may discontinue the monthly transfer of delinquent Mortgages at any time
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon written notice to the Transferor Servicer and may provide up to 180 days’ notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to the discontinuation of the monthly transfer of delinquent Mortgages if requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferor Servicer must cooperate with Freddie Mac and is responsible for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Facilitating, at its expense, the transfer of the Mortgage file, payment history, Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: account, copies of all correspondence with the Borrower and any other information
elements.label: text

elements.text: and records or documentation pertaining to the Mortgages requested by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Providing the Borrower with any notice of the change with respect to the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: activities as may be required by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Informing any necessary third parties, such as tax and insurance services, attorneys,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and vendors of the change with respect to the Servicing activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Remitting compensatory and other fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may be assessed compensatory and other fees (e.g., failing to comply with Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s EDR requirements, late foreclosure sale reporting compensatory fees or State
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure timeline compensatory fees) associated with its failure to comply with certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requirements as provided under the Guide. Such fees will be billed to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer on the Servicer’s monthly Servicer Non-Performing Loans Invoice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Remitting payments via an ACH draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must remit payment of Servicer Non-Performing Loans Invoices via an ACH
elements.label: text

elements.text: draft initiated by Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac will initiate the ACH draft on the last
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day of the month in which the Servicer receives the Servicer Non-Performing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans Invoice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To authorize ACH drafting of Servicer Non-Performing Loans Invoices or to make
elements.label: text

elements.text: changes to ACH draft account instructions previously provided, Servicers must complete,
elements.label: text

elements.text: execute and submit a Form 1132, Authorization for Automatic Transfer of Funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: Through the Automated Clearing House (ACH) for Servicers, no later than 15 Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days before the last Business Day of the first month in which the Servicer owes any
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory and other fees to Freddie Mac reflected on the Servicer Non-Performing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans Invoice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must deliver Form 1132 to Freddie Mac as an Electronic Record (as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 1401.1(b)), using a Portable Document Format (PDF) (or other Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Record format commonly used in the mortgage industry), that has been completed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: contains the copy or representation of the pen and ink signature of the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Employee (such copy or representation of the Authorized Employee’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: signature shall be such Authorized Employee’s adopted Electronic Signature as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 1401.1(b)) and either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Attach to an e-mail and deliver to Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: CashCollections@FreddieMac.com; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upload through the Freddie Mac eBill system
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and the Servicer agree that the delivery of Electronic Records with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Signatures are eligible Electronic Transactions (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401.1(b)) and are governed by the applicable provisions of Chapter 1401.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The employee authorized to execute Form 1132 on the Servicer’s behalf must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated as an “Authorized Employee” on the Servicer’s Form 988SF, Certificate of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Incumbency for a Bank, Savings Bank, Savings and Loan Association, Credit Union,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation or Limited Liability Company, or Form 989SF, Certificate of Incumbency
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Limited Partnerships, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Establishing an ACH draft account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The account the Servicer designates as the ACH draft account must not be a Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account. Servicers may establish one ACH account for the payment of invoices for both
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Performing Loans Monthly Servicer Billing Statement and the Servicer Non￾Performing Loans Invoice or establish two separate accounts. Servicers may
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit billing inquires or billing-related requests to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer_Billing@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Sections 8303.1(d) and 8303.5(b) concerning Performing Loans remittances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The term “Performing Loans Invoices” on Form 1132 includes, but is not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Performing Loans monthly Servicer Billing Statements” and “PL001 (Performing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans).” The term “Nonperforming Loans Invoices” on Form 1132 includes, but is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, “Servicer Non-Performing Loans Invoices” and “NPL01 (Non Performing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans).”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Receiving reimbursement of expenses/payment of incentives via ACH credit entries
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be reimbursed for expenses and paid incentives (see Section 9204.3(a) regarding Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensation for alternatives to foreclosure), Servicers must authorize Freddie Mac to make
elements.label: text

elements.text: such payments via ACH credit entries into their commercial checking accounts. See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2405.1(a) for information on authorizing receipt of ACH credit entries or making changes to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ACH credit account instructions previously provided.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may submit ACH credit-related questions or concerns to
elements.label: text

elements.text: 104_Expense@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should refer to Chapter 9701 for detailed information related to the reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: of expenses and the use of PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Before a Servicer may receive expense reimbursements and incentive payments via
elements.label: text

elements.text: ACH credit entries, the Servicer must have completed the certificate of incumbency (COI)
elements.label: text

elements.text: process requirements in Section 2201.1, and Freddie Mac must have accepted the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: COI and all other required forms and documents. The employee authorized to execute Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1132 (the “ACH Authorization”) on the Servicer’s behalf must be designated as an
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Authorized Employee” on the Servicer’s Form 988SF or 989SF, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must deliver Form 1132 to Freddie Mac as an Electronic Record (as defined

elements.text: (ii) Establishing an ACH draft account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The account the Servicer designates as the ACH draft account must not be a Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account. Servicers may establish one ACH account for the payment of invoices for both
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Performing Loans Monthly Servicer Billing Statement and the Servicer Non￾Performing Loans Invoice or establish two separate accounts. Servicers may

elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Receiving reimbursement of expenses/payment of incentives via ACH credit entries
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be reimbursed for expenses and paid incentives (see Section 9204.3(a) regarding Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensation for alternatives to foreclosure), Servicers must authorize Freddie Mac to make
elements.label: text

elements.text: such payments via ACH credit entries into their commercial checking accounts. See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2405.1(a) for information on authorizing receipt of ACH credit entries or making changes to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ACH credit account instructions previously provided.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 104_Expense@FreddieMac.com.

elements.text: Servicers should refer to Chapter 9701 for detailed information related to the reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: of expenses and the use of PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools).

elements.label: text

elements.text: ACH credit entries, the Servicer must have completed the certificate of incumbency (COI)
elements.label: text

elements.text: process requirements in Section 2201.1, and Freddie Mac must have accepted the Servicer’s

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Late charge assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Late charge collection
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Late charge assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not assess a late charge in an amount greater than 5% of the principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest payment. On an FHA or VA Mortgage, a late charge may be assessed and collected
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with the applicable program terms. The Servicer is responsible for collecting
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Borrower any recording or similar costs incidental to the granting of relief.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may not impose a late charge if the Mortgage is a Home Mortgage and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: installment is received within 15 days after the Due Date. If this date is a Saturday, Sunday
elements.label: text

elements.text: or federal holiday, it is extended to the next Business Day. The Servicer may retain any late
elements.label: text

elements.text: charge collected as additional Servicing compensation if the charge is allowed under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Late charge collection
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of any provision to the contrary in the Note or the Security Instrument, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may not collect a late charge by any of the following methods:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Charging the Borrower’s Escrow or impound account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deducting from a regular monthly installment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deducting from a payment made to partially or fully cure a Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adding to the outstanding principal balance of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Causing a Mortgage to become delinquent or be placed in foreclosure because of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: unpaid late charge
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For payment application requirements, refer to Sections 8103.3(a) through 8103.3(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9102.3: Establishing Borrower contact during
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Delinquency (08/13/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Collection techniques
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Quality right party contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Collection techniques
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of a delinquent Mortgage must be based upon personal contact, either by
elements.label: text

elements.text: telephone, e-mail or face-to-face interviews with a Borrower. Form letters and notices, while
elements.label: text

elements.text: having a place in any Servicing program, generally are not as effective as personal contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must not be used exclusively.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s collection techniques must include the use of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Telephone contacts or face-to-face interviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written communications such as notices and letters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other responsible collection techniques as permitted under applicable law including, but
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to, e-mail, text messaging, voice response unit technology or a Servicer’s web
elements.label: text

elements.text: portal
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer discovers that the Borrower’s contact information (phone number or mailing
elements.label: text

elements.text: address) is invalid, then it should initiate skip trace activities to obtain alternate phone
elements.label: text

elements.text: numbers or mailing addresses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Quality right party contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer’s objective in contacting a delinquent Borrower is to establish quality right party
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Quality right party contact defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: Quality right party contact occurs when a Servicer establishes contact with the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: and discusses with the Borrower, co-Borrower or trusted advisor, such as a housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: counselor, the most appropriate options for Delinquency resolution. A Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: make every attempt to achieve quality right party contact by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining the reason for the Delinquency and whether the reason is temporary or
elements.label: text

elements.text: permanent in nature
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining the occupancy status of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining the Borrower’s ability to repay the debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Setting payment expectations and educating the Borrower on the availability of
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternatives to foreclosure as appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtaining a commitment from the Borrower to either resolve the Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: through traditional methods (paying the total delinquent amount) or engaging in an
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure solution
elements.label: text
